programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: A hyperactive Persian cat suddenly collapses while playing, and Jackson sees if a pet that has been exiled from the family home is ready to come back indoors

programme.title: Muhammad Ali
programme.sub-title: Round Four: The Spell Remains - Part Two
programme.desc: The conclusion of the documentary about the boxer and activist reveals how he retired from fighting in 1981 at the age of 39, before being diagnosed with Parkinson's in 1984. The programme examines how he continued to travel the world spreading his Islamic faith, becoming a symbol of peace and hope, as well as his final two fights and his famous lighting of the Olympic torch at the 1996 Olympics

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.sub-title: Chessmen v Rugby Boys: Semi-Final Two
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts the second semi-final of the quiz as the Chessmen take on the Rugby Boys, making connections between things that initially do not appear to be linked. One set of clues comprises Carmilla, Neo, Lord Voldemort and Sir Leigh Teabing

programme.title: Art of America
programme.desc: Andrew Graham-Dixon explores the role of artists and architects in the development of American cities and culture, as the country sought to step out of Europe's shadow. He travels to Manhattan, Chicago and Massachusetts to examine the work of Norman Rockwell, John Sloan, George Bellows and Louis Sullivan, before charting the links between the Great Depression of the 1920s and 30s and the rise of abstract expressionism. He also visits Jackson Pollock's studio in Long Island and Mark Rothko's Chapel in Houston, Texas

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The second round of the student quiz continues, with two more teams of four pitting their wits against one another as they attempt to secure a place in the quarter-finals. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.sub-title: Chessmen v Mathematicians: The Competition for Third Place
programme.desc: Three chess players challenge the Mathematicians in a bid to win third place in the quiz show of patience and lateral thinking, where successful contestants make connections between things that initially do not appear to be linked. Victoria Coren Mitchell presents

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth is waiting for a new three-piece suite to be delivered, boasting it is an exact replica of the one at Sandringham House - and goes to extraordinary lengths to ensure her neighbours see the royal warrant on the delivery van. But, as ever, her family contrives to take the gloss off her moment of glory. Comedy, starring Patricia Routledge

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth decides to take Daddy out for a picnic, but her plans soon backfire. Suburban comedy, starring Patricia Routledge, Clive Swift and Geoffrey Hughes

programme.title: A History of Britain by Simon Schama
programme.desc: Simon Schama investigates how the noble ideals behind the British Empire failed to match the reality. While British society believed it was delivering the benefits of modern Western civilisation, in truth the inhabitants of India and Ireland suffered privation due to a laissez-faire economic policy. Mutiny, famine and lack of religious sensitivity drove a wedge between Britons and Indians, while the great potato famines of the 1840s caused mass migration from Ireland to America, Canada, Australia and New Zealand

programme.title: Muhammad Ali
programme.sub-title: Round Three: The Rivalry - Part One
programme.desc: A look at how the boxer faced his fiercest rival Joe Frazier in the 'Fight of the Century' as he attempted to come back from exile and regain his heavyweight title. Ali also became a focal point for Americans who were growing increasingly disillusioned with the war in Vietnam

programme.title: Muhammad Ali
programme.sub-title: Round Three: The Rivalry - Part Two
programme.desc: The Supreme Court overturns the boxer's conviction, validating his refusal to serve in the war in Vietnam. In 1974 he takes on his greatest rival Joe Frazier in a highly anticipated rematch, but to complete his comeback he must face the fearsome George Foreman

programme.title: Muhammad Ali
programme.sub-title: Round Four: The Spell Remains - Part One
programme.desc: The boxer shocks the world when he defeats George Foreman in the Rumble in the Jungle in Zaire, winning back the heavyweight title and becoming one of the most famous men on Earth. He then faces Joe Frazier for a third time in the brutal Thrilla in Manila

programme.title: Ken Loach Remembers - Up the Junction
programme.desc: Ken Loach on Up the Junction, his landmark 1965 contribution to the BBC's The Wednesday Play strand, adapted from Nell Dunn's celebrated stories of working-class life in Battersea. Blending drama with documentary realism, the production shocked audiences with its frank depiction of sex, illegal abortion and everyday poverty, attracting record complaints while redefining television drama in Britain

programme.title: Up the Junction: The Wednesday Play
programme.desc: A well-to-do girl leaves her parents and comfortable home in fashionable Chelsea to sample life in working-class Battersea, but is not prepared for the culture shock awaiting her. Drama, starring Carol White, Geraldine Sherman and Vickery Turner. Directed by Ken Loach

programme.title: The Sky at Night
programme.desc: The team examines the threat of space weather, meeting some of the dedicated people working to keep the Earth safe from the very worst that the Sun can throw at it. Chris Lintott meets Jim Wild, the president of the Royal Astronomical Society, learn about the Carrington Event of 1859, when a massive solar storm caused aurorae as far south as the Caribbean. Maggie Aderin drops into Warwick University to discover how Dr Ravindra Desai's research is helping people grasp how the matter spat out by the sun interacts with the Earth

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.sub-title: Chessmen v Mathematicians: The Competition for Third Place
programme.desc: Three chess players challenge the Mathematicians in a bid to win third place in the quiz show of patience and lateral thinking, where successful contestants make connections between things that initially do not appear to be linked. Victoria Coren Mitchell presents

programme.title: Art on the BBC
programme.desc: Art historian Rose Balston explores six decades of BBC archive to discover how TV has influenced people's understanding of John Constable. The artist pushed the boundaries of landscape painting, to the extent he was rejected by the establishment. But perhaps just as surprisingly, he poured his love for one woman on to the canvas as no painter of landscapes had done before

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The second round of the student quiz concludes, with two more teams of four pitting their wits against one another as they attempt to secure a place in the quarter-finals. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.sub-title: Cambridge Quiz Society v Rugby Boys: The Final
programme.desc: Rugby players compete against a trio from the Cambridge Quiz Society in the final of the quiz show where successful contestants make connections between things which at first do not appear to be linked. Victoria Coren Mitchell presents

programme.title: Universe
programme.desc: Professor Brian Cox begins his epic exploration of the cosmos, beginning with the great luminous bodies that bring light and warmth to the universe, the stars. Throughout the opening episode, he looks at how stars created life and meaning in the cosmos, from the dawn of time through to the arrival of our own star, the Sun

programme.title: Laurence Rees Remembers - The Nazis: A Warning from History
programme.desc: Historical filmmaker Laurence Rees looks back on his acclaimed 1997 documentary series, explaining its origins and how the fall of the Berlin Wall opened possibilities for interviews with people who previously had been unreachable

programme.title: The Nazis: A Warning from History
programme.desc: Account of Hitler's rise to power, featuring archive film from Russia and eyewitness accounts by former Nazi party members. The first programme concentrates on the Nazi party's formation after the First World War, the growth of support in the late 1920s and the eventual Fuehrer's appointment as Chancellor in 1933

programme.title: Ken Loach Remembers - Old Oak
programme.desc: Ken Loach reflects on The Old Oak, his deeply moving portrait of solidarity, displacement and community in a former mining village in County Durham. Set against the arrival of Syrian refugees in a town scarred by austerity and deindustrialisation, the film explores whether compassion and collective action can survive in fractured times

programme.title: The Old Oak (2023)
programme.desc: TJ Ballantyne was born and bred in his village in Country Durham and he has never left the close-knit community, becoming a valued leader following the 1984 miners strikes. He owns The Old Oak, the last remaining pub. The arrival of a bus of Syrian refugees exposes division and despair, leading to an ugly confrontation between 20-something new arrival Yara and a local man. TJ risks the wrath of old friend Charlie to help Yara. Ken Loach's drama, starring Dave Turner

programme.title: Arena
programme.sub-title: Clint Eastwood - Part Two: American Filmmaker
programme.desc: Part two of two. Profile of Clint Eastwood, examining his lifelong commitment to the process of film-making. The actor made his directorial debut with the 1971 thriller Play Misty for Me, which proved the foundation for a career encompassing classics such as The Outlaw Josey Wales, Bird and Unforgiven. With contributions from Gene Hackman, Meryl Streep and Dina Ruiz

programme.title: This Cultural Life
programme.desc: Director Ken Loach talks to John Wilson about the key moments in his life that helped shaped his creativity. From his Midlands childhood as the son of a factory worker and annual summer holidays in Blackpool to his love for Czech cinema, which would become a huge inspiration on his own career. He also discusses his films for television, including Up the Junction and Cathy Come Home

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.sub-title: Cambridge Quiz Society v Rugby Boys: The Final
programme.desc: Rugby players compete against a trio from the Cambridge Quiz Society in the final of the quiz show where successful contestants make connections between things which at first do not appear to be linked. Victoria Coren Mitchell presents

programme.title: Universe
programme.desc: Professor Brian Cox begins his epic exploration of the cosmos, beginning with the great luminous bodies that bring light and warmth to the universe, the stars. Throughout the opening episode, he looks at how stars created life and meaning in the cosmos, from the dawn of time through to the arrival of our own star, the Sun

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Strictly dancer Amy Dowden turns detective, travelling to west Wales to investigate a rumour of murder in her family. She is shocked to discover that an ancestor died when she was just 14 and investigates how it happened on a farm in 1888. On her dad's side, Amy wants to uncover what led to her grandfather being the only one adopted out of six children. The cause of death of her great grandmother also comes as a painful shock to Amy and she learns about a tale of bravery and hardship in a working-class mining family

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: A look ahead to the latest forecast

programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Doorbell Detectives
programme.desc: Matt Allwright hears from a Doorbell Detective who was robbed in the night by two men while she was asleep upstairs. Plus, a teenage hero whose camera captured the moment he rescued an elderly lady from a burning building

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts sees the warning signs directly spelt out to him at a Merseyside property. Jacqui Joseph meets four friends in Suffolk embarking on their first property project. And Dion Dublin is in Yorkshire where he wants to fill a void

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp is in her home city of Glasgow, where she visits the world-famous Burrell Collection - 9,000 rare antiques and artworks that were donated to the city in 1944. Danny Sebastian and Roo Irvine help the teams

programme.desc: The drugs unit arrest a trafficker on a cruise ship linked to the deadly drug carfentanyl in a major bust. Officers respond when a woman reports her phone has been stolen by her estranged boyfriend at a luxury golf course, and the police are on hand to assist at a tourist bike crash

programme.title: Robson Green's Weekend Escapes
programme.desc: Robson joins up with Sheli McCoy, AKA Gladiator Sabre, to explore her home area of Fife. A paddleboard tour of the coast is followed by a farm stay overnight. The morning sees them embark on a road trip to Anstruther, where they take a boat trip to the nature reserve of the Isle of May. Famous for its bird life, fauna and flora, the pair attempt to capture the scene with landscape artist Liz Myhill

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps Lisa swap Northampton for a Powys life reboot with her children and takes to a canoe, 128 feet above the ground

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Lee Burkhill and Flo Headlam head to Winchester, Hampshire, to transform Stefan and Lucinda's unloved plot into a garden inspired by their love of making meals outdoors. The pair want to see a 'kitchen' in the garden and a spot to grow produce that they can cook on a barbecue. All this needs to be achieved on a £6,000 budget

programme.title: Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Friends Phoebe, Katie, Sam and Cal aim to win big by stepping in the right direction, avoiding the wrong answers and making it across the bridge to the other side. Game show, hosted by Ross Kemp

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Roman Kemp present another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Kat plans a birthday dinner for Jasmine, Oscar wrestles with the feelings he has for both Josh and Jasmine, and Jack tries to get through to Denise

programme.title: Interior Design Masters with Alan Carr
programme.desc: The two finalists head to Longleat Estate to transform rental cottages, taking inspiration from either the nearby safari park or from the surrounding countryside. They aim to show judges Michelle Ogundehin and Laurence Llewelyn-Bowen how much their design ability has evolved during the competition before one of them wins Interior Design Masters and a partnership with retailer Next for their own homewares range

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Actor and TV presenter Joe Swash traces the history of his father's family, who he lost at the age of 12, knowing only that they supposedly came from Italy. Using documents given to him by his mother and the help of experts, he discovers stories of murder, banditry, a political activist and a family bar in southern Italy

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: A round-up of today's headlines

programme.title: Spotlight
programme.desc: Investigations into social and political issues affecting Northern Ireland

programme.title: England 2006: The Golden Generation
programme.desc: Documentary exploring why the men's England football team did not fulfil their potential at the 2006 FIFA World Cup. Sven-Goran Eriksson's talented side was captained by the talismanic David Beckham, but the players and their partners came under increasing scrutiny off the pitch, and they were eventually eliminated by Cristiano Ronaldo's Portugal. With contributions from their key members of the squad, including Rio Ferdinand, Wayne Rooney, Ashley Cole, Owen Hargreaves and Paul Robinson

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: Pip, Ravi and Connor form a plan that might finally bring their hunt for Jamie to an end, but first Pip must swallow her pride and ask Cara for help. In a daring breaking-and-entering mission, Pip faces the person behind Jamie's disappearance and when a complicated truth emerges, she's forced to accept the line between good an evil is a murky one

programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Doorbell Detectives
programme.desc: Phil returns home to find someone hanging around his house. When he checks his doorbell footage, he sees the burglar drop some incriminating evidence

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.sub-title: Well Worth the Wait in Cornwall
programme.desc: An old Sunday School in Cornwall undergoes a transformation and two unique properties in Hampshire and Lancashire are up for auction

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Danny Sebastian hosts a special show all about tea and the history of tea-drinking. In Eastbourne, he is serenaded by organist Chris Mannion as they reminisce about tea dances, while in Hastings Danny is given a 'tea-tastic' masterclass by etiquette expert Rebecca Gildea. At the end of the show, he counts down the most profitable tea-related antiques and collectibles

programme.title: Policing Paradise
programme.desc: Officers find a haul of suspected cannabis plants is found at a residential home, stolen bike parts are discovered in a derelict hotel, and dogs sniff out drugs in three bags coming in on a flight from New Jersey at the airport

programme.title: Robson Green's Weekend Escapes
programme.desc: Robson takes fellow actor Jo Joyner horse riding near the small town of Wooler as he introduces her to his beloved Northumberland. The Cheviots provide a stunning backdrop at the riding centre at Kimmerton - where Jo gets back in the saddle for the first time in years. With her love of riding renewed, Robson takes Jo to Heatherslaw Mill for an outdoor cookery lesson to make soda bread

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah helps a potential buyer to find a home in the Lake District, presenting cottages in some of the region's most sought-after villages, including a unique mystery house

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Flo Headlam and Joe Swift are in Wilmslow to help Vin and Reena transform their chaotic patch of grass, gravel and concrete into a zen and grow-your-own garden for family healing with a budget of £4,000

programme.title: Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Quiz show hosted by Ross Kemp in which contestants compete for cash by crossing a bridge made up of stepping stones, each of which has a different option. Friends Mark, Sheila, Giselle and Shaun must try to get from A to B by stepping on the truths and avoiding the lies in order to cross the studio floor safely, or risk falling off the bridge and out of the game

programme.desc: JB Gill and Angellica Bell present the magazine show, with celebrity guests and stories of interest from around the UK

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ian seeks redemption, Nicola is determined to help George, and the Slaters gather for the birthday dinner

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Experts restore a life-sized model horse used as a teaching aid at a riding school for the disabled, a chair that survived the Second World War, a handmade globe that reveals what time it is anywhere around the world and a leather darts case

programme.title: Amandaland
programme.desc: Amanda hosts a prom pre-party at her flat for Georgie and her friends, but Felicity gets in the way, the make-up designer is terrible and the limo gets stuck

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: The diggers move in at the allotments, forcing the protest group to take extreme action. With time running out for Richard, he decides to lay it all on the line for Emily - but will it prove enough?

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: A round-up of today's headlines

programme.title: Pitch Invasion: How the USA Changed Soccer
programme.desc: How soccer in the USA would experience a second coming, with the likes of David Beckham heading to States, after the boom and bust of the 1980s. By the late 1970s, the NASL was continuing to attract the biggest footballing stars in the world, with new innovations making the sport attractive to its American audience. However, the good times didn't last, and the NASL was in a dire financial situation by the mid-1980s, seeing the end of the league, as the money and interest dried up. However, the college system helped fuel the rise of the US Women's National Team, whose World Cup and Olympic triumphs transformed women's football globally, while Beckham's arrival in MLS in 2007 reignited the men's game before Lionel Messi took Inter Miami and MLS to new heights in 2023

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: Pip, Ravi and Connor return to the abandoned manor hoping for answers but the arrival of two mysterious figures leads to a dangerous chase. The verdict is given in court sooner than anyone expected and at school, Pip loses control earning herself a suspension. A crucial clue cracks the case wide open

programme.title: Blankety Blank
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh hosts the comedy quiz show in which a panel of six celebrities fill in the missing blanks and help win some brilliant prizes for some very lucky contestants. This week, he is joined by Julian Clary, Sophie Willan, Harry Pinero, Liza Tarbuck, Judi Love and Tom Allen

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Charlie Cooper's Myth Country
programme.desc: The writer and actor heads to Wiltshire, home to ancient sites such as Stonehenge, Avebury and the Westbury chalk horse, to get to the bottom of why humans feel the need to leave marks on the landscape. He turns his investigation towards the far more recent phenomenon of crop circles, hoping to settle a debate that has plagued his family for years. They think that crop circles are the work of aliens, but Charlie strongly doubts that belief

programme.title: Charlie Cooper's Myth Country
programme.desc: The actor travels to Wales, the land of dragons and song, on a mission to discover if King Arthur was a real man or just a myth and if there's any truth behind this treasure story. In the process, he meets treasure hunters of different sorts, a recorder of rare and ancient folk songs, and finds what he refers to as 'his tribe', a metal-detecting club from south Wales. He's optimistic he will return home a rich man - if not in wealth, then certainly in knowledge

programme.title: High: Confessions of an Ibiza Drug Mule
programme.desc: Michaella explains she was faced with a dilemma - stick to her story about being forced to carry the drugs and face 15 years in jail, or come clean and break her mother's heart

programme.title: High: Confessions of an Ibiza Drug Mule
programme.desc: Despite being sentenced to nearly seven years in a maximum security prison, Michaella was home in less than three. She reveals how she got out early and how the experience changed her for the better

programme.title: Brickies
programme.desc: Molly realises her career aspirations will only be reached if she can overcome her fear of driving, and the brickies have a heavy night out watching England v Germany in Euro 2020

programme.title: Brickies
programme.desc: The builders head to a village in Derbyshire, where a retaining wall is needed to protect a housing development from possible land slips and flooding. Multi-talented Lauren is drafted in to help give things a push, but she is not impressed by some of the attitudes on site. Lucas experiences setbacks on and off site as he confesses that his relationship is on the rocks. After struggling to get to grips with a new method of bricklaying, he pulls on the boxing gloves and enjoys sparring with Lauren

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: Animated short film about an old-fashioned video-game shop owner in 1995 who faces competition from a slick rival with the latest console in stock. With the voices of Emily Atack and Jamie Demetriou

programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's news

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall heads to a place very few people ever go, Snake Island in Brazil, where the concentration of snakes is higher than anywhere else on earth

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall heads to Slovenia in search of the nose-horned viper and also hopes to track down a bear and goes deep into a cave system to find a dragon

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The three hosts are each given a budget of £5,000 to find a car that can hold its own on a racetrack and handle everyday tasks, before testing their purchases in Germany. James May puts two Porsches through their paces on the Top Gear test circuit, and former Downing Street communications director Alastair Campbell tries out the Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Louis Theroux: My Scientology Movie (2015)
programme.desc: The reporter investigates the controversial religious organisation, but finds himself denied access to anyone involved. Adopting a different approach, he resorts to recruiting actors to re-enact the personal stories of former members - only to find his project is under surveillance by representatives of the church

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The presenters search for the world's best four-seat supercar, pitting the Aston Martin Rapide against the Porsche Panamera Turbo and Maserati Quattroporte in a series of unusual challenges in the south of England. Harry Potter star Rupert Grint and ex-Formula One driver Rubens Barrichello each do a lap in the Reasonably Priced Car and Richard Hammond puts the Chevrolet Camaro SS and Mercedes E63 AMG through their paces on the test track

programme.title: Charlie Cooper's Myth Country
programme.desc: The writer and actor tours the UK in his campervan, exploring local legends, myths and folklore and gathering evidence in a bid to get to the truth. He begins by going in search of Black Shuck, the demon dog of East Anglia, attempting an overnight stakeout to capture it on film, with unexpected consequences

programme.title: Ladhood
programme.desc: Liam examines his relationship with drugs after he finds himself doing cocaine at a warehouse party with a stranger on a Tuesday night, when he should be preparing for his mental health assessment the next day

programme.title: Ladhood
programme.desc: Liam lies to Jess about missing his mental-health assessment and tensions in their relationship reach breaking point, leaving him suddenly nostalgic for the simplicity of first love

programme.title: Peacock
programme.desc: Liz's disappearance has left an empty space at the gym which Andy and Jay compete to fill - and with his body positivity classes struggling and his relationship drifting, Andy cannot afford to lose. Comedy, starring Allan Mustafa

programme.title: Peacock
programme.desc: Andy starts his new job at a different gym but is soon spooked by high-energy fitness influencer Kara's talk of high standards. What will become of his long-distance bromance with Spooner?

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: A short comedy film about sleep deprivation, the brutality of the music industry and the power of Iggy Pop's hip thrust

programme.title: Louis Theroux: My Scientology Movie (2015)

Adopting a different approach, he resorts to recruiting actors to re-enact the personal stories of former members - only to find his project is under surveillance by representatives of the church

programme.title: Live Athletics
programme.desc: The Bislett Games. Coverage of the sixth meeting of the campaign, held at Bislett Stadium in Oslo, Norway. George Mills set a new British record of 12:46.59 in the 5,000m here last year, but still had to settle for fourth place behind American victor Nico Young and Ethiopians Biniam Mehary and Kuma Girma, There was also a win for Young's compatriot Isabella Whittaker in the 400m, who beat home favourite Henriette Jaeger into second place, with Team GB's Amber Anning third and Ireland's Rhasidat Adeleke fourth

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder

In a daring breaking-and-entering mission, Pip faces the person behind Jamie's disappearance and when a complicated truth emerges, she's forced to accept the line between good an evil is a murky one

programme.desc: The survivors of a deadly game designed by a sinister corporation are dragged back into an even deadlier trap. And as a handful of former escapees join in, the competitors must work together to outsmart their inhuman tormentors and give their all to solve increasingly sophisticated puzzles. Horror sequel, starring Taylor Russell and Logan Miller

programme.title: Ladhood
programme.desc: Liam is back at his parents' house in Leeds intending to prepare for a performance at a local spoken-word night, but instead procrastinates and plays old video games. But when he turns on his childhood computer and stumbles on an old home-made hip-hop track he created with his teenage mates, it triggers a memory

programme.title: Small Town, Big Riot
programme.desc: Mobeen Azhar's investigation reveals a white supremacist that was campaigning outside the Suites hotel ahead of the protest may have influenced the riot. He also heads further afield to Scampton, Lincolnshire where residents have been campaigning against the Home Office's planned asylum centre in the former RAF base

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The three hosts are each given a budget of £5,000 to find a car that can hold its own on a racetrack and handle everyday tasks, before testing their purchases in Germany. James May puts two Porsches through their paces on the Top Gear test circuit, and former Downing Street communications director Alastair Campbell tries out the Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: Two emotionally unassembled young men start a play group, Funboys Fun Club, for childish young men to engage in some wholesome mucking about. When they come across severely depressed 60-year-old Marty, they try their best to bring some joy back to his life. Pilot for the sitcom, with Rian Lennon and Ryan Early

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: Pip, Ravi and Connor return to the abandoned manor hoping for answers but the arrival of two mysterious figures leads to a dangerous chase. The verdict is given in court sooner than anyone expected and at school, Pip loses control earning herself a suspension. A crucial clue cracks the case wide open

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: Pip, Ravi and Connor form a plan that might finally bring their hunt for Jamie to an end, but first Pip must swallow her pride and ask Cara for help. In a daring breaking-and-entering mission, Pip faces the person behind Jamie's disappearance and when a complicated truth emerges, she's forced to accept the line between good an evil is a murky one

programme.title: Ali & Ava (2021)
programme.desc: Devoted mother and grandmother Ava, a teaching assistant with a love of country and folk music, is offered a lift home from school by the charismatic Ali, who is estranged from his wife but continuing with the charade of marital domesticity for the sake of their close-knit relatives in Bradford. On the journey, they find many things in common, including a love of music, and begin an unusual and secretive courtship. Romantic drama, starring Adeel Akhtar and Claire Rushbrook

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Adam Henson are in Lisburn, Northern Ireland, for the 157th Balmoral Show, the region's largest agricultural and food event, welcoming more than 100,000 visitors each year. Adam explores heritage crafts that the community are determined to preserve and joins families showing their prize livestock, while Sean is in charge of the stopwatch at the Tractor handling competition

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham, Michaela Strachan and the team continue to broadcast live from the National Trust's Crom Estate as nature writes its own stories. The live remote cameras are capturing fledging, predation and drama around the clock, from otters on the lough shore to red squirrels in ancient woodlands. Iolo Williams takes a look at the lives of some of the country's rarest species at the Knepp rewilding estate in Sussex

programme.title: Cher Meets Rylan
programme.desc: The singer and actress chats to the Radio 2 presenter about her album Christmas, her biggest hits and her incredible life and career. Throughout the show there is archive footage from across the decades, illustrating Cher's glittering music career, her success in film, the many memorable fashion choices, and how she has influenced so many

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: David Harper presents from one of Britain's biggest antiques fairs in Detling, and brings along a Victorian mystery object. Mark Stacey and Kate Bliss assist the teams

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Chris Hull and Diarmuid Gavin are in Oxford to help Adam, Vaneesha and her father, Graham, transform their two triangular, mirror-image gardens each with a patio and a solitary shed into a family-friendly space, with a budget of £6,000

programme.title: Farm 999
programme.sub-title: Badger Baiters Tracked and Race Against Wildfire
programme.desc: In Northern Ireland, a devastating wildfire tears through the Glens of Antrim, threatening farmland, homes and livestock. As flames spread across the hills, the fire service battle to save buildings, animals and livelihoods. With a drone team and the Irish Air Corps called in, it becomes a race against time to stop the blaze before it consumes everything in its path. In Richmond, North Yorkshire, police close in on a brutal gang of badger baiters, after a tip-off from a local Rural Watch group leads officers to a car full of suspects and a horrific crime scene

programme.title: Lost and Found in the Lakes
programme.desc: The detectorists climb the fells at Crummock Water in blistering heat, hoping to find a bracelet given to a heartbroken daughter by her late father. The freedivers are out in force searching Ullswater for a precious pendant and jeweller Nisha turns old damaged coins into treasure again. Presented by Helen Skelton

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Tennis: Queen's
programme.desc: The HSBC Championships. Coverage of day two of the women's grass-court tournament at Queen's Club, featuring matches from the first round of the singles competition. British hope Emma Raducanu was in fine form at this stage last year, overcoming Cristina Bucsa 6-1, 6-2. Her compatriots Sonay Kartal, Heather Watson and Katie Boulter also progressed to round two, defeating Daria Kasatkina, Yulia Putintseva and Ajla Tomljanovic respectively

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Simon Armitage, Josie Gibson, Ardal O'Hanlon and Adele Roberts test their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo hunts for truffles in the Istrian countryside near the hilltop town of Buzet, assisted by Lila, an expert labrador. In Rijeka, he discovers the history of a military coup led by an Italian poet in the aftermath of the First World War. Michael finishes on the moonscape Island of Pag, where he helps to make the salty cheese for which the island is famed

programme.title: Marcus Wareing Simply Provence
programme.desc: Marcus is challenged to a live cooking demo at the market and has a VIP dinner guest to impress with traditional local produce

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham and Michaela Strachan present the annual series charting the fortunes of British wildlife during the changing of the seasons

programme.title: Pitch Invasion: How the USA Changed Soccer
programme.desc: How superstar players such as Pelé and George Best made America fall in love with soccer in the 1970s, as an entertaining new game emerged, in contrast to English football, which was associated with crumbling stadiums, hooliganism and a grim atmosphere. The New York Cosmos signed Pelé on what was then the biggest contract in sporting history, while Best saw the USA as a chance to reinvent himself and the game. Riding this wave, the NASL boldly predicted global dominance by 1985 - but sadly it was not to be

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.sub-title: River Itchen, Hampshire - Wild Brown Trout
programme.desc: Bob Mortimer and Paul Whitehouse return with a focus on people, places and rivers to which they have personal connections, beginning with Paul taking Bob to a rarely fished spot on the River Itchen in Hampshire, an unspoilt part of this unique chalk stream. Paul also catches up with old friend James Murray, who is leading a campaign to save special rivers such as this for future generations, discussing how they may be able to help reduce the dramatic decline in salmon

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Victoria Derbyshire

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: The Mother of All Cons
programme.desc: In 2016, the parents-turned-sleuths have tracked Megan and Jean down to Disney World in Florida and are still questioning how ill the teenager really is. They can see that people are still donating to the fundraising campaign, many of them also parents of sick children, and so they resolve to raise the alarm that Meg might not be ill at all, but then a tragedy makes them question everything they have uncovered

programme.title: The Chronicles of Armagh
programme.desc: Autumn continues, with apples being harvested just before unprecedented flooding wreaks havoc, while clear skies bring celestial wonders for the astronomers

programme.title: The Chronicles of Strangford
programme.desc: It is summer and Mark is camping out in the hope of seeing some early morning bird activity, while Katie is searching for barn owl chicks

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The finalists jet off to the Caribbean where they are set three challenges designed to broaden their culinary horizons like never before. For starters, they prepare a barbecue feast for locals, before having a shot at local fine dining at the Estate house within the five-star Jumby Bay Island Resort, with each of them preparing a dish from the dinner menu. They finish off with another task at Jumby Bay - working as private chefs for a VIP dinner, where guests include cricketing legend Viv Richards

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: The pressure builds as chicks prepare to fledge and predators close in. The team hope to capture even more intimate moments from the lives of the wildlife calling National Trust Crom home through their 24-hour remote camera surveillance. Away from Crom, Iolo Williams spends his final night live at Knepp, where rewilding is transforming the landscape

programme.title: Surgeons: At the Edge of Life
programme.desc: A surgeon must remove his patient's voice box to save his life, while another takes on a life-changing procedure for a patient with a rare auto-immune condition

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Briony May Willams helps an air ambulance pilot to find a rural home that is within an hour of Belfast airport, and also has a go at Irish dancing by Lough Neagh

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Chris Hull and Lee Burkhill travel to Coventry to help mother-and-daughter nurses Barbara and Tate transform their completely empty garden into an exotic jungle retreat

programme.title: Great Japanese Railway Journeys
programme.sub-title: Japanese Alps: Nagano to Narai Juku
programme.desc: Michael Portillo ends the first leg of his journey on a Shinkansen bullet train, heading to Nagano, the host city of the 1998 Winter Olympics and the gateway to the Japanese Alps. In the town of Obuse, Michael learns how Japan's most famous artwork, Hokusai's The Great Wave off Kanagawa, was made. He visits the largest wasabi farm in the world, and heads to the historic mountain town of Narai Juku, with its beautifully preserved wooden houses

programme.title: Great Korean Railway Journeys
programme.desc: On the final leg of his journey, Michael Portillo travels to South Korea's south west, where food, protest and landscape reveal the values that have shaped the modern nation. Arriving by high-speed train in Gwangju, he explores the region's deep-rooted food culture. Later, the presenter confronts a darker chapter in South Korea's history at the May 18th Memorial Park. Leaving the mainland, Michael crosses by ferry to Jeju Island, a volcanic landscape dominated by Mount Halla

programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Tennis: Queen's
programme.desc: The HSBC Championships. Coverage of day three of the women's grass-court tournament at Queen's Club, featuring singles matches from the second round. The top four seeds enter the competition at this stage, including the top-ranked Jessica Pegula, and her American compatriot Amanda Anisimova, who was runner-up both here and at Wimbledon last year. The other two who received byes are talented Canadian teenager Victoria Mboko and Olympic gold medallist Belinda Bencic of Switzerland

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Simon Armitage, Josie Gibson, Ardal O'Hanlon and Adele Roberts test their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo's railway adventure reaches the Dalmatian coastal city of Zadar, once an important Venetian trading hub and still a centre for the production of Maraschino cherry liqueur, a Venetian delicacy. He visits a renowned tailor to try on a traditional Dalmatian suit in the medieval backstreets of Trogir. At the Cathedral of St Domnius, Michael learns about the importance of Catholicism to the Croatian people

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Ben Robinson explores more villages around the UK, starting with Runswick Bay in North Yorkshire, where he discovers how this popular tourist site was once a place of danger. Benvisits the caves in the sea cliffs known as hob-holes to uncover how they once helped supply a thriving jet industry and finds evidence of how, in the late-1800s, the village became the inspiration for a group of artists who became known as the British Impressionists

programme.title: The Future with Hannah Fry
programme.desc: Investigating whether revolutionary technology can transform the lives of millions of disabled people, with a look at coffee making robots in Japan and bionic limbs in New York

programme.title: The Secret Genius of Modern Life
programme.desc: Refrigerators have been quietly 'keeping things cool' in the kitchen for over 100 years, and yet most people barely give them a second thought. Hannah Fry visits the production line at Bosch to find the lifeblood at the heart of every fridge, and gets a lesson in carrot droop

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham and Michaela Strachan celebrate the season's standout wildlife moments. Iolo Williams continues his exploration of the seabird colonies at Bempton Cliffs, where thousands of gannets, guillemots and puffins crowd the towering coastline

programme.title: Surgeons: At the Edge of Life
programme.desc: A surgeon has one chance to tackle a life-threatening tumour in his patient's liver and diaphragm. A neurosurgeon must save her patient from paralysis with unerring precision

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Victoria Derbyshire

programme.desc: Investigating the crime happening in plain sight on high streets across Britain - from illegal cigarettes hidden in the back room of a minimart in Rochdale to cocaine being bought over the counter from a shop in the West Midlands. Also, exposing the crime gangs exploiting the vulnerable and setting up shop in Britain

programme.title: Unspun World with John Simpson
programme.desc: The BBC's world affairs editor reflects on the week's major global news stories, talking to local experts from around the world to go behind the headlines

programme.title: The Heart of Me (2003)
programme.desc: A bohemian artist in 1930s England falls for her handsome brother-in-law - and when he makes his illicit intentions clear, they begin a passionate affair, unaware the eventual discovery of their betrayal will lead to trauma and tragedy. Period drama based on Rosamond Lehmann's novel, starring Helena Bonham Carter, Paul Bettany, Olivia Williams and Eleanor Bron

Back at the station, Anna and Perri can't resist the temptation to hide the boss's car, temporarily. Meanwhile, the whole team leap into action when a stolen car fails to stop

programme.desc: Unforgettable WWE moments focusing on the Survivor Series of the 2000s

programme.desc: David Dickinson and his team assess items brought in by members of the public in Liverpool. They have the choice of taking the cash for their antiques or gambling at auction

programme.desc: David Dickinson and his team head to Wood Green in north London to help people generate cash from their unwanted belongings. Antiques expert Ian Towning doesn't know what to make of a Victorian stereo-viewer and David Ford sees double with a porcelain girl and matching print. Plus, one seller gains a lot of cash for her gold

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: Rick grapples over a decision when a WWE Hall of Fame ring hits the store

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: A 1906 silver half dollar that belonged to a passenger on the Titanic is brought into the shop

programme.title: Money for Nothing

programme.title: Junk & Disorderly: Henry's Wreck-trospective
programme.desc: Compilation of episodes from the show Junk & Disorderly, which features Henry Cole and other restorers buying, fixing, and selling automotive junk

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.desc: On an uncharacteristically wet day in Montebello, King Darrell is on a mission to defend his throne. However, Rene and Casey signal their intentions to undermine his plans. Kenny gets bowled over by his locker, while Lisa manages to rope herself something quite impressive

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.desc: Barry shows up to the auction ready to learn, bringing along a present for a certain hustler. Brandi and Bozek get a firm grip on a smaller unit that raises their spirits, while Ivy treats his son to a birthday locker

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.desc: In Riverside, Lisa dons her cowboy garb and brings her money sock along. Rene sinks his teeth into an unassuming unit, Brandi gets to go on a scavenger hunt, Ivy and his boys get a glimpse of retired life and Darrell just isn't feeling like himself

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.desc: San Bernardino is full of surprises, particularly for Rene and Casey, who roll the dice on some sealed totes. Kenny stumbles over some odd-looking car parts, while Lisa shields herself from disaster with an unusually expensive locker

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.desc: Ivy is on the warpath in Lancaster, doing his best to intimidate the other buyers, and he won't be losing any sleep over it. Brandi begrudgingly lets Bozek decide which locker they buy, while Dusty goes up against the King to prove a point

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.desc: Barry utilises a little-known tactic to snatch up a locker, and it ties him up in knots. Brandi shops in the bargain basement but puts on quite a show in the process. Ivy lands a large unit and the tools stack right up

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.sub-title: Two for the Price of Dust
programme.desc: The big guns come out in Riverside, but Rene isn't having any of it. Darrell presses his luck with a smaller locker and makes a neat profit. Dusty finds that once he gets going, it's hard to stop - so he just keeps re-loading

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.sub-title: It's a Safe Time to Get Lit!
programme.desc: Lisa heads to Huntington Beach, Darrell spends the right amount of time going through his locker, Kenny rides a recent string of luck right into his unit, while Rene puts his bankroll up against a pair of safes

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: Rick checks out a rare 1589 manual for war practices and fortifying defences after it is brought into his store

programme.desc: Experts examine the link between aliens and the Civil War, exploring Abraham Lincoln's strange visions and ghostly images seen at Gettysburg

programme.title: Craig Charles: UFO Conspiracies

programme.title: Emergency
programme.desc: Surgeons Brett and Pete use bone hooks and levers to try pull paratrooper Lee's pelvis out of his spinal canal after a high-speed paragliding accident in France. In south-west London, 86 year-old Veronica comes into St George's Hospital with an open ankle fracture after a car accident but doctors worry there's a vascular injury and she could lose her foot. At St Mary's, Heidi has surgery after a vicious dog attack and 56 year-old plasterer Lee is brought in by air ambulance after a car crash where emergency doctors took over an hour to extract him from the vehicle

programme.title: Gypsy Rose: Life After Lock Up
programme.desc: Gypsy is told that Ryan will be listed as her baby's legal father until a court-ordered paternity test is completed. Social media gossiping doesn't help the situation

programme.title: Gypsy Rose: Life After Lock Up
programme.desc: As Gypsy awaits paternity test results, she plans a 'Babymoon' with Ken to revive their relationship amid social media scrutiny

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Chef Gordon Ramsay heads to the Park's Edge restaurant in Atlanta, Georgia, where a series of spats with the local community are hampering its success. Owners Richard and Jorge's in-fighting has alienated locals, and the duo's suggestion that customer numbers are low because of racism has heightened tensions

programme.title: Falling
programme.desc: Anna feels out of place and struggles to settle back into old routines, seeking guidance from a trusted confidant. David begins to find his feet again, helping out old friends. But as he wrestles with his true feelings, Susan encourages him to take action and face his fears. Romantic drama, starring Keeley Hawes and Paapa Essiedu

programme.title: Falling
programme.desc: As Susan's wedding approaches, David must figure out what it really means to let love in. Anna seeks a fresh start, undeterred by the challenges, and finding support and encouragement from an unlikely source. But as they both embrace change, will their paths bring them together?

Also, Mercedes has a life-altering decision to make

programme.desc: Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Mark Chapman in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against words and numbers

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Rebecca attempts to impress the corporate bigwigs by promoting Norm's painting business, while Sam's sporty girlfriend leaves him exhausted. Comedy, starring Ted Danson

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Frasier and a pregnant Lilith reject civilisation and move to the wilderness to enjoy a more natural way of life, while Woody is forced to take his first holiday since he came to work at the bar

programme.desc: Debra invites her divorced parents to Thanksgiving dinner, and Raymond sees something shocking that convinces him they are getting back together. The truth, however, proves far stranger. Robert Culp and Katherine Helmond guest star

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray is annoyed to discover Marie is plying his brother with gifts of scented candles and massage oils. However, his mood changes when he deduces her real motive - to put Robert in a romantic mood, in the hope he will provide her with grandchildren

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Amy's brother Peter turns to Ray for advice on talking to women, while Debra decides to help out by throwing a party and inviting all her single friends

programme.sub-title: Shut Out in Seattle - Part Two

programme.desc: Martin and Frasier inadvertently offer to pay for Daphne's wedding, while Niles sinks into gloom as the marriage approaches and tries to console himself by dating someone new - unaware she is a prostitute. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Ten-year-old Henry is brought to Queen's Medical Centre in Nottingham after being hit accidentally by a forklift truck driven by his dad. X-rays confirm that both his legs are broken. Nahum and Dean arrive by ambulance following a road traffic accident, and 94-year-old Pearl comes in after badly cutting her arm by trapping it in a lift

programme.title: Building Monaco's Superhomes
programme.desc: Luxury property developer Guy Phoenix embarks on a rollercoaster journey of buying and renovating a £16million Monaco apartment: the biggest investment and financial risk he has ever taken. As Guy makes the first steps towards finding the perfect Monaco apartment to renovate, things go wrong from day one, he quickly discovers that building in one of the most expensive places to live on the planet is anything but easy, as local planning restrictions and impossibly high building costs threaten to turn his lifelong dream of owning a luxury apartment in Monaco into a nightmare. To make matters worse, his £16million apartment doesn't even include a parking space

programme.title: In the Footsteps of Killers
programme.desc: Emilia Fox, David Wilson and Dr Graham Hill investigate the disappearance of Georgina Gharsallah who left her mum's house in March 2018 and was never seen again. The case is now being treated as a murder investigation and an examination of the circumstances of Georgina's disappearance leads the team to a shocking conclusion

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Chef Gordon Ramsay heads to Mike & Nellie's Steakhouse in Oakhurst, New Jersey, where owner Mike Farber is mourning his father Nellie, who was also his business partner. Having been called in by the restaurateur's two daughters, Gordon faces the task of getting him to focus on his business, before tackling the bigger problem of rotting decor and a less-than-appealing menu

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In the Lake District, Paul and Glenda kick off the competition in their static caravan, but eyebrows are raised at his unromantic approach to his relationship. On day two self-confessed gadget geek David and his partner Jane show their competitive side with a confidently presented, well-received meal. Finally outdoorsy twentysomethings Matt and Polly show off their 'good life' approach to cooking with a home-grown feast

programme.title: Dispatches
programme.sub-title: Will My Summer Holiday Be Cancelled?
programme.desc: Kate Quilton investigates the uncertainty over summer holidays, ever since the Iran War sent jet fuel prices soaring and caused thousands of flights to be cancelled. She finds out whether foreign holidays are now out of the question or if the opposite is true, with bargains to be had. She also explores whether holidaying in the UK could be a smarter alternative.

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: The architect meets a couple planning to transform a 1960s ambulance into a campervan, and an artist who has commissioned a giant wooden floating egg to use as a work and living space. programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: An offhand comment to the twins' new friend has dire consequences for Ray, who cannot understand why everyone is so upset - but he compounds his error by accidentally repeating the remark to the boy's father. Ray Romano stars

programme.desc: The station shock-jocks decide to play practical jokes - and Frasier's pompous manner makes him the ideal target for their dubious humour. Starring Kelsey Grammer and David Hyde Pierce

programme.desc: Martin returns from a friend's funeral upset by how it was conducted, and begins to plan his own memorial. Meanwhile, sacked DJ Bulldog takes the opportunity to embark on an entirely new career - in pizza delivery. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Niles and Frasier fall out, each convinced the other is secretly coveting his date. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and David Hyde Pierce

programme.title: American Pickers

programme.title: Car S.O.S.

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray and Rachel Riley host the long-running words-and-numbers game with Mark Chapman joining Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jean Johansson jets off to the eastern Costa del Sol in southern Spain to help Joanne and Darren from south Wales find the perfect holiday home for them and their family. Darren, a former Mr Wales bodybuilding champion, is especially looking forward to more outdoor exercise in the warm weather. They have a budget of £180,000 for their dream property, so hope Jean can find one appealing enough for them to make an offer

programme.title: Narrow Escapes
programme.desc: Peter and Noni swap their beloved narrowboat Barbarella for a hire boat, as they set course for the epic Falkirk Wheel, the world's only rotating boat lift. Josh, Hayley and their family celebrate a special birthday afloat, while Tom embarks on his maiden voyage from Nantwich on the Shropshire Union Canal, venturing beyond the marina and into new waters. And a canal infrastructure emergency needs the Canal & River Trust team's urgent attention

programme.title: Chateau DIY: Australia
programme.desc: At Château de la Fare, Janet and Philip reach dizzying heights in their stairwell renovation. In Nouvelle-Aquitaine Denise and Brett tackle an en-suite and near Périgueux, Lia and Richard attempt to get their kitchen party-ready.

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: The Rodriguez sisters help fitness-mad couple Kris and Ali relocate to Spain full-time and Darren and Natalie Brown help married couple Terry and Maria find a new home

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer catch up with a couple in Edinburgh, and one in Derbyshire, almost 20 years after their search with the dream duo, which means they've had plenty of time for life to move on in wonderful ways. Kirstie and Phil first searched with Steph and Tana in Edinburgh in 2020, and although one house really stood out for them, its proximity to Edinburgh Airport's flightpath put them off. After the search they took Kirstie and Phil's advice on board and broadened their search, and four years on the pair find them in a house where they weren't afraid to get stuck in themselves. Wendy and Simon asked for Kirstie and Phil's help in Derbyshire way back in 2007, when they needed a house that could also accommodate their businesses - Wendy's as a fashion designer and Simon's as a photographer. They had an offer accepted, which ultimately fell through, but five years later they were in a three-bed cottage in Darley Abbey. They decided to keep work and home separate, so were renting a business unit nearby and work was flourishing. Fast forward another 12 years and it's all change again - a new house, two children and three photographic studios!

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud follows a couple who face a race against time to realise their dream. Two years ago, corporate accountant Pep was diagnosed with terminal cancer. But rather than rest and avoid stress, Pep and partner Malene focused their attention on a long-held ambition to build a new house on their land in the Surrey Hills. The plan is to construct a modern take on a Viking hall, inspired by their love of all things Scandinavian and Malene's Danish heritage. But they must build quickly so Pep can enjoy what time she has left in the finished home

programme.title: Britain's Most Expensive Houses
programme.desc: On north London's prestigious Billionaires' Row, Jeremy Fine is selling Fair Hill, a £20 million mansion steeped in royal history. Spanning 13,900 square feet, it boasts six reception rooms, eight bedrooms and a nightclub-style party room. He enlists international buying agent Nick Dawson to gauge interest from his billionaire clients. In Hertfordshire, Margaret Towney helps serial property flippers Mark and Linda turn a profit on Asterley House - previously lived in by Vinnie Jones - while simultaneously hunting for their next dream home. Could Cheryl Cole's former residence be the ideal solution?

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: A rolling cast of famous faces, including the likes of Vernon Kay and Paddy McGuinness, Denise Van Outen and Johnny Vaughan, Vicky Pattison and Pete Wicks, Bez and Shaun Ryder, Ashley Banjo and Perri Kiely, Rylan and mum Linda, and Roman and Martin Kemp sit down to give their sharp, insightful and funny thoughts on the week's TV

programme.title: Building Monaco's Superhomes
programme.desc: Property developer Guy Phoenix buys and renovates a £16million apartment in Monaco, the biggest investment and financial risk he has ever taken. In this second of two programmes, as he navigates the intricacies of building in one of the most expensive places to live on the planet, Guy comes under pressure to complete his luxury apartment in time for the upcoming Monaco Grand Prix. As the finish line gets closer, the pressure and stress on both him and his crack team of builders becomes almost unbearable

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay travels to Fremont, California, in an attempt to save a restaurant that is tearing the owners' marriage apart. Saki Kavouniaris took over Spin a Yarn in 1995 and began to turn it into a success, before marrying bartender Jennifer. When the business started to fail, the couple decided to splash out on refurbishments to recover their glory days, but their efforts have been futile and now their relationship is at stake

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In
programme.desc: Joe Lycett is joined by Alan Davies to sample an action-packed weekend in Trieste, northern Italy. Trieste is famous for the Bora, the north-easterly wind said to shape the city and its inhabitants. Joe and Alan discover this at the Wind Museum, where curator Rino presides over a collection of wind samples donated from all over the world. A few miles north the pair enjoy a food and fizz pairing in the village of Prosecco. Fortunately, they're in the right place for hangovers as Trieste is Italy's caffeine capital. No mini break is complete without learning a new skill to brag about when you get home, so Joe and Alan attend a painting class

programme.title: Rosaline (2022)
programme.desc: A member of an aristocratic family is dumped by her suitor Romeo in favour of her own cousin Juliet. Outraged at the betrayal, she sets out to sabotage the most famous love story in history. Romantic comedy, starring Kaitlyn Dever and Isabela Merced

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Three couples compete for the £1,000 prize in Swindon, where Carla and Hayley kick off the competition by serving up a spicy Caribbean-themed menu for food snob Jeanette and her husband Rob, and lovebirds Tim and Kath. However, spice-loving Carla causes upset by revealing she plans on bringing her own hot pepper sauce to the other couples' dinner parties

programme.title: In the Footsteps of Killers
programme.desc: Emilia Fox, David Wilson and Dr Graham Hill investigate the disappearance of Georgina Gharsallah who left her mum's house in March 2018 and was never seen again. The case is now being treated as a murder investigation and an examination of the circumstances of Georgina's disappearance leads the team to a shocking conclusion

programme.title: Gypsy Rose: Life After Lock Up
programme.desc: As Gypsy awaits paternity test results, she plans a 'Babymoon' with Ken to revive their relationship amid social media scrutiny

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Chef Gordon Ramsay heads to the Park's Edge restaurant in Atlanta, Georgia, where a series of spats with the local community are hampering its success. Owners Richard and Jorge's in-fighting has alienated locals, and the duo's suggestion that customer numbers are low because of racism has heightened tensions

programme.title: Falling
programme.desc: Anna feels out of place and struggles to settle back into old routines, seeking guidance from a trusted confidant. David begins to find his feet again, helping out old friends. But as he wrestles with his true feelings, Susan encourages him to take action and face his fears. Romantic drama, starring Keeley Hawes and Paapa Essiedu

programme.title: Falling
programme.desc: As Susan's wedding approaches, David must figure out what it really means to let love in. Anna seeks a fresh start, undeterred by the challenges, and finding support and encouragement from an unlikely source. But as they both embrace change, will their paths bring them together?

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien are in Norfolk to meet upcycler and bike enthusiast Simon. The items they uncover include a filing cabinet, a trunk and a classic bicycle

programme.title: Hollyoaks Omnibus
programme.desc: Dee Dee struggles to hide her emotions as the village gathers for Diane's funeral, Beth continues to seek justice, and a resident's trauma catches up with them. A teenager's grief leads them down a dangerous path, Warren has a surprise for Mercedes, and Beth sets in motion a plan that sends shockwaves through feuding families. Also, Mercedes has a life-altering decision to make

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Mark Chapman in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against words and numbers

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Lilith and Frasier use Carla's six-year-old son as a subject on which to practise their parenting skills. Bar-room comedy, guest starring Jarrett Lennon, with Kelsey Grammer, Bebe Neuwirth and Rhea Perlman

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Rebecca attempts to impress the corporate bigwigs by promoting Norm's painting business, while Sam's sporty girlfriend leaves him exhausted. Comedy, starring Ted Danson

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Frasier and a pregnant Lilith reject civilisation and move to the wilderness to enjoy a more natural way of life, while Woody is forced to take his first holiday since he came to work at the bar

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra invites her divorced parents to Thanksgiving dinner, and Raymond sees something shocking that convinces him they are getting back together. The truth, however, proves far stranger. Robert Culp and Katherine Helmond guest star

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray is annoyed to discover Marie is plying his brother with gifts of scented candles and massage oils. However, his mood changes when he deduces her real motive - to put Robert in a romantic mood, in the hope he will provide her with grandchildren

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Amy's brother Peter turns to Ray for advice on talking to women, while Debra decides to help out by throwing a party and inviting all her single friends

programme.title: Frasier
programme.sub-title: Shut Out in Seattle - Part One
programme.desc: Part one of two. Daphne's engagement to Donny leaves Niles feeling down, so he seeks solace in the arms of a younger woman. Frasier keeps getting his girlfriend's name wrong, Martin's relationship with Bonnie turns sour, and Roz cannot bring herself to break up with Bulldog

programme.title: Frasier
programme.sub-title: Shut Out in Seattle - Part Two
programme.desc: Part two of two. Niles and Roz take extreme action to solve dilemmas in their respective love lives. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer, David Hyde Pierce and Peri Gilpin

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Martin and Niles meet the new woman in Frasier's life - and discover to their surprise that she looks just like his mother. Comedy, guest starring Rita Wilson, alongside Kelsey Grammer, John Mahoney and David Hyde Pierce

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Martin and Frasier inadvertently offer to pay for Daphne's wedding, while Niles sinks into gloom as the marriage approaches and tries to console himself by dating someone new - unaware she is a prostitute. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: The collectors go hog wild at a sprawling ranch in Texas, where Mike Wolfe flips for a VW bus that has been transformed into a hunting lodge on wheels

programme.desc: At Château de la Fare, Janet and Philip reach dizzying heights in their stairwell renovation. In Nouvelle-Aquitaine Denise and Brett tackle an en-suite and near Périgueux, Lia and Richard attempt to get their kitchen party-ready.

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: The Rodriguez sisters help fitness-mad couple Kris and Ali relocate to Spain full-time and Darren and Natalie Brown help married couple Terry and Maria find a new home

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer catch up with a couple in Edinburgh, and one in Derbyshire, almost 20 years after their search with the dream duo, which means they've had plenty of time for life to move on in wonderful ways. Kirstie and Phil first searched with Steph and Tana in Edinburgh in 2020, and although one house really stood out for them, its proximity to Edinburgh Airport's flightpath put them off. After the search they took Kirstie and Phil's advice on board and broadened their search, and four years on the pair find them in a house where they weren't afraid to get stuck in themselves. Wendy and Simon asked for Kirstie and Phil's help in Derbyshire way back in 2007, when they needed a house that could also accommodate their businesses - Wendy's as a fashion designer and Simon's as a photographer. They had an offer accepted, which ultimately fell through, but five years later they were in a three-bed cottage in Darley Abbey. They decided to keep work and home separate, so were renting a business unit nearby and work was flourishing. Fast forward another 12 years and it's all change again - a new house, two children and three photographic studios!

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud follows a couple who face a race against time to realise their dream. Two years ago, corporate accountant Pep was diagnosed with terminal cancer. But rather than rest and avoid stress, Pep and partner Malene focused their attention on a long-held ambition to build a new house on their land in the Surrey Hills. The plan is to construct a modern take on a Viking hall, inspired by their love of all things Scandinavian and Malene's Danish heritage. But they must build quickly so Pep can enjoy what time she has left in the finished home

programme.title: Britain's Most Expensive Houses
programme.desc: On north London's prestigious Billionaires' Row, Jeremy Fine is selling Fair Hill, a £20 million mansion steeped in royal history. Spanning 13,900 square feet, it boasts six reception rooms, eight bedrooms and a nightclub-style party room. He enlists international buying agent Nick Dawson to gauge interest from his billionaire clients. In Hertfordshire, Margaret Towney helps serial property flippers Mark and Linda turn a profit on Asterley House - previously lived in by Vinnie Jones - while simultaneously hunting for their next dream home. Could Cheryl Cole's former residence be the ideal solution?

programme.title: Building Monaco's Superhomes
programme.desc: Luxury property developer Guy Phoenix embarks on a rollercoaster journey of buying and renovating a £16million Monaco apartment: the biggest investment and financial risk he has ever taken. As Guy makes the first steps towards finding the perfect Monaco apartment to renovate, things go wrong from day one, he quickly discovers that building in one of the most expensive places to live on the planet is anything but easy, as local planning restrictions and impossibly high building costs threaten to turn his lifelong dream of owning a luxury apartment in Monaco into a nightmare. To make matters worse, his £16million apartment doesn't even include a parking space

programme.title: Tip Toe
programme.desc: When Clive meets Diane at work, the chance of a romance leads him to confess secrets that he's kept quiet for years. But when Clive and Leo discover the truth about Calico Road, they're thrown into open warfare. Marie, Saul and George have no choice but to join the battle, as the two households conspire against each other. But Clive's fury won't be stopped, and the danger to Leo gets closer

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay travels to Fremont, California, in an attempt to save a restaurant that is tearing the owners' marriage apart. Saki Kavouniaris took over Spin a Yarn in 1995 and began to turn it into a success, before marrying bartender Jennifer. When the business started to fail, the couple decided to splash out on refurbishments to recover their glory days, but their efforts have been futile and now their relationship is at stake

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In
programme.desc: Joe Lycett is joined by Alan Davies to sample an action-packed weekend in Trieste, northern Italy. Trieste is famous for the Bora, the north-easterly wind said to shape the city and its inhabitants. Joe and Alan discover this at the Wind Museum, where curator Rino presides over a collection of wind samples donated from all over the world. A few miles north the pair enjoy a food and fizz pairing in the village of Prosecco. Fortunately, they're in the right place for hangovers as Trieste is Italy's caffeine capital. No mini break is complete without learning a new skill to brag about when you get home, so Joe and Alan attend a painting class

programme.title: Rosaline (2022)
programme.desc: A member of an aristocratic family is dumped by her suitor Romeo in favour of her own cousin Juliet. Outraged at the betrayal, she sets out to sabotage the most famous love story in history. Romantic comedy, starring Kaitlyn Dever and Isabela Merced

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Three couples compete for the £1,000 prize in Swindon, where Carla and Hayley kick off the competition by serving up a spicy Caribbean-themed menu for food snob Jeanette and her husband Rob, and lovebirds Tim and Kath. However, spice-loving Carla causes upset by revealing she plans on bringing her own hot pepper sauce to the other couples' dinner parties

programme.title: Sarah Beeny's New Life in the Country
programme.desc: The family enhance their new greenhouse to make it well and truly part of their home, as it turned into a sanctuary for Sarah to escape to during her chemotherapy treatment. Sarah and Graham want to create warmth for people as well as plants, so they transform the greenhouse into a welcoming outdoor room complete with an antique wood burner. Alongside the greenhouse transformation, the family also put on a chicken rehoming event

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay revisits three restaurants he helped that were among the biggest challenges he faced, finding out whether they stayed on the road to recovery or turned back into nightmares. First he meets sisters Rita and Lisa to see if they have continued serving fresh instead of frozen food at Boston's La Galleria 33, before heading to the Olde Hitching Post in Hanson, Massachusetts, to discover whether controlling owner Tom has kept his promise and handed over the reins to his daughter Andrea. Finally, Gordon goes back to the Prohibition gastro pub in Everett, Washington, to catch up with owner Rishi and learn if she has ditched belly-dancing in favour of running her business

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to the Prohibition Grille in Everett, Washington, where he meets belly dancer-turned-restaurateur Rishi Brown, who is making all the wrong moves with her business. programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: An outbreak of food poisoning at Krusty Burger is traced to barley produced in a nearby town of Norwegian immigrants and results in the mills being shut down, so the out-of-work residents venture to Springfield to find new jobs. programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA

Can Gordon get through to this stubborn owner and save the eatery from disaster?

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: There is scepticism, competition and high emotions when Gloria wins a bid at the school auction for a family seminar with famous doctor and author Debra Radcliffe, whose self-help book, Clean Out Your Junk Drawer, encourages people to get out all their emotional junk to become healthier people and better partners and spouses

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Gloria rents a cabin in the mountains in which she hopes she and her family will be able to enjoy a picture-perfect white Christmas. programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Marge starts her own sandwich shop after her snacks become a huge hit at Springfield Elementary. However, running a business proves challenging - especially when the same franchise opens another location across the street

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Eco-disaster looms in Springfield when Mr Burns launches a controversial fracking operation - and while Homer is more than happy to play accomplice to his boss, an outraged Lisa calls on the services of bigshot politician Maxine Lombard (voiced by two-time Oscar winner Jane Fonda) to shut Burns down. Against the odds, however, the opponents end up falling for one another

programme.title: The Simpsons
