programme.title: Apache Uprising (1965)
programme.desc: Western, starring Rory Calhoun as a tough-talking cowboy with a heart of gold who bravely defends fellow stagecoach passengers from a potentially lethal Indian attack. Also starring Corinne Calvet, Lon Chaney Jr and John Russell

programme.title: Arrowhead (1953)
programme.desc: A cavalry scout witnesses a series of raids by an Apache tribe that is supposedly in peace talks - but his superiors reject his story out of hand and dismiss him from duty. Continuing to spy on the tribe, he comes to suspect the chief's son is behind the attacks. Western, starring Charlton Heston, Jack Palance and Katy Jurado

programme.title: Star Trek
programme.desc: Kirk's reunion with an old friend turns sour thanks to the presence of a group of Klingons. When the captain learns the warrior race has meddled in a primitive society, he has to decide whether to ignore Starfleet orders and attack, or keep a low profile and allow the aliens to wreak havoc. William Shatner stars

programme.title: The Six Million Dollar Man
programme.sub-title: One of Our Running Backs Is Missing
programme.desc: Steve finds himself caught up in a battle of the giants when a professional footballer is kidnapped. Seventies drama, starring Lee Majors

programme.title: Babylon 5
programme.desc: A damaged alien craft is brought on board the ship, and the crew identifies its inhabitant as the Soul Hunter, an immortal predator that can sense death

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: The Rangers are given six hours to rescue a billionaire's daughter before she is killed live on the Internet. Adventure with the modern-day lawman whose crime-solving methods are rooted firmly in the Old West. Chuck Norris stars

programme.title: Money Train (1995)
programme.desc: A New York cop in debt to loan sharks persuades his foster brother and fellow police officer to rob a train carrying all the day's takings from the city's subway network. However, things start to go wrong when a female detective creates rivalry between the pair, and a pyromaniac serial killer threatens their plans. Action comedy, starring Woody Harrelson, Wesley Snipes, Jennifer Lopez and Chris Cooper

programme.title: Striking Distance (1993)
programme.desc: A cop refuses to accept the suspect convicted for murdering his father is the real perpetrator of the crime. Meanwhile, his career derails and he is demoted to river patrol. However, his instincts prove accurate as the real criminal resurfaces, hurling him into a race against time to save other innocent lives. Thriller, starring Bruce Willis, Sarah Jessica Parker, Dennis Farina and Tom Sizemore

programme.title: Hot Seat (2022)
programme.desc: A reformed hacker finds himself in the hot seat when he realises that he's just armed a bomb. Action adventure, starring Mel Gibson

programme.title: Burning Rubber (2019)
programme.desc: A veteran motor racing driver is persuaded to come out of retirement, but ends up in direct competition with his own son, who has signed up with a rival team. Drama, starring John Travolta, Shania Twain and Michael Madsen

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Paramedics rush to save a grandmother with severe osteoporosis who fell down the stairs carrying her four-month-old grandson. In Stratford, paramedics Mandy Morrice and Michelle Godwin treat a racegoer on a night out who collapsed in the street and suffered a serious head wound. In Warwick, a concerned member of the public calls emergency services to a suspected cardiac arrest but ends up becoming the patient himself. Plus, Neil Vann and Laura Perkins give life-saving treatment to an elderly man found unconscious face down in his hallway

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: Air ambulance crews respond to headteacher Andy who has collapsed after a cardiac arrest in Essex and warehouse employee Rob who is trapped under a pallet of granite in Ipswich. Katie, who is 13-weeks pregnant, calls for help when she keeps having seizures and the Essex and Herts Air Ambulance crew help Jo who has been lying on the bathroom floor with a broken ankle for nine hours

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A woman is airlifted to hospital after falling off her mobility scooter and getting knocked unconscious. Her daughter and husband discuss how she has led an active life despite being diagnosed with a crippling illness at a young age. A 78-year-old man is rushed into A&E after falling down the stairs, and doctors fear he may have a bleed on his brain

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The wife, mother and daughters of a man who has been hit by a car wait for news from the doctors of whether he has suffered internal injuries, including a bleed on the brain. Other patients include a man who has cut off four of his toes mowing the lawn, and a three-year-old boy who has suffered a seizure

programme.title: Supermarkets Unwrapped
programme.desc: The Food Unwrapped team take a deep dive into the nation's favourite supermarket aisles. In this first episode, the team chill out in the freezer aisle. Jimmy Doherty investigates why there's more to frozen chips than just potatoes, Helen Lawal learns why frozen ready meals have best-before dates, Briony May Williams gets the inside scoop on soft-scoop ice cream, and Andi Oliver learns how to get the most out of your freezer at home

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The researchers investigate the life and work of a Glaswegian author who had lost all contact with his family, and take a trip to the home of a newspaper man from Norwich and uncover a previously unknown pension pay out of £30,000

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien head to Staffordshire to meet petrolhead Martin, who's also the organiser of a local music festival

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: A quartet of B&B owners battle it out to decide which of their establishments provides the best value for money, beginning with luxury weight-loss and detox retreat Slimmeria in East Sussex. The guests worry about uncomfortable sleeping arrangements, the price of accommodation and the strict 9pm curfew - and the prospect of a dance-style workout only exacerbates their bad mood

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Jan and William Cheeseman, owners of five-star Gold-rated 38 St Giles in Norwich, try to impress their competitors. Unfortunately, poor breakfast presentation and complaints about noise from the street increase the tension between the couple as they strive for perfection

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Ann and Bill Clay play host at their four-star Bosvean House B&B in Bude, Cornwall. Diane and Max Seivewright query the establishment's luxury description, while Galia Grainger complains about the lack of a sea view. However, the hosts hope an indulgent Cornish cream tea, a stick dance and a drink in their garden shed will succeed in winning over the guests

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Village bakers Diane and Max Seivewright, the owners of Ombersley B&B in Worcestershire, host the last round of the competition, but some guests are far from impressed with the rooms and food

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners gather to find out who has won the competition. Diane and Max Seivewright, the hosts at Ombersley B&B, feel betrayed by Ann and Bill Clay's feedback and want to know why they have been so harshly criticised, while Galia Grainger asks why Slimmeria's rules and regulations have provoked so much hostility

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The first host in Bournemouth, 38-year-old bar manager and musician Adie, hopes to hit the right notes by ensuring his night of laid back fun is full of music and laughter. With trumpets and drums at the ready and tricky crustaceans proving hard to crack, does Adie have what it takes to win the £1,000 prize?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The second Bournemouth host is aristocratic vet Sir Henry, who plans to charm his way to the prize by hosting an evening of relaxed dining

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Countryside-loving Pippa from Bournemouth hopes to impress her guests by throwing a dinner party with every course inspired by fish

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Bournemouth, spiritual student Simone hopes to wow her guests with a spicy Bajan menu. She's keen to impress her new friend, posh vet Sir Henry, but she's unsure about Pippa, who doesn't hold back as she probes Simone about her student house

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the last night in Bournemouth, flamboyant charity manager Jacqueline hopes that Michelin star-style food will win her the prize. With partridge and black pudding scotch eggs, this could be a culinary night to remember, but will Sir Henry and Simone steal her thunder with an unlikely romance?

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition kicks off at St Hilary Guest House in Llandudno, north Wales, where owner Tricia Watson and her daughter Jess hope that their classic seaside destination will impress. Guests Darren and Alex take a shine to their sink, but Debbie and Paul aren't pleased with the plumbing. At dinner, there's food for thought when some of the guests' culinary expertise is revealed, leaving the hosts in the hot seat. And there's a surprising revelation from Sue and Linda. At breakfast, cooking methods and quality are questioned, before a refusal to return has Tricia and Jess chewing over their feedback

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second of this week's destinations is The Falcon Retreat in south Wales, run by Sue Burston and her right-hand woman Linda Roberts. When the guests arrive at the secluded location, Darren and Alex are wowed by the view, but there are some issues in their room. At dinner, the guests are surprised by their hosts' previous professions, and a conversation about the additional charge for breakfast worries Sue and Linda

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week takes place at the Crown Inn in Grewelthorpe, North Yorkshire, where owners Debbie Heartfield and Paul Dixon hope to impress their guests

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final venue is Stix Glamping in Greetham, Rutland, where one guest decides glamping is not for her before she has even set foot on site, leaving her partner all alone

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Payment Day sees the B&Bers take each other to task on everything from insufficient slumber to hopeless hosting, before the payments are revealed and we find out who's won

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp searches for a family home around Hove on the south coast of England for Duncan, Anna and their three children, as they return from seven years living in Switzerland. Elsewhere, Phil Spencer helps couple Mick and Bernie look for a property to downsize to when they retire in the South Downs

programme.title: PopMaster TV
programme.desc: Ken Bruce hosts the penultimate episode of the series, and the last chance for players to secure a place in the grand final. Who has the best pop knowledge and will go through?

programme.title: Ancient Greece by Train with Alice Roberts
programme.desc: On the island of Paros, in the middle of the Aegean Sea, Alice joins an archaeologist on a tour of the ancient sites he has discovered in 38 years of digging. Alice takes a boat to the island of Delos where the Temple of Apollo once stood, and leaves Greece behind to go to Türkiye, where Ancient Greece was once the prevalent culture

programme.title: Castle Secrets
programme.sub-title: The Secret Escape; Tower of Torture; A King's Untamed Lust
programme.desc: A look at the fortress where a knight was trapped, while his nemesis lay patiently waiting. This edition also explores a sprawling fortress, which was the site of a dynasty's gruesome and inventive form of punishment. Plus, examining a king's palace, which was home to the shady secrets of all his marital failures

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A 63-year-old builder falls from a great height while working on a construction site, and his daughter shares her stories of the sacrifices he made for her. Doctors fear a teenager who has been hit by a car may have sustained brain injuries, while a man who collapsed while playing golf talks about his unconventional upbringing in Kenya and how he had to adapt to fulfill his dreams

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A teenager with anorexia nervosa is rushed into the paediatrics emergency department with dangerously low blood pressure. Her mother reflects on her daughter's lifelong struggle with her eating disorder, and the pain she feels at being unable to protect her child. A 66-year-old man who fell from a great height onto a concrete surface is brought in to St George's, and doctors fear he has sustained organ damage and internal bleeding. His daughter talks about their close relationship and the sacrifices her father has made over the years

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A carpenter is rushed to St George's after partially severing his hand with a power saw, a farmer is kept under observation after puncturing his lung in a quad-bike accident, and a man suffers an unexpected electric shock from a heart implant that was installed after he survived a stab wound. He and his girlfriend recall the attack, and how it changed both their lives

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Paramedics rush to save a grandmother with severe osteoporosis who fell down the stairs carrying her four-month-old grandson. In Stratford, paramedics Mandy Morrice and Michelle Godwin treat a racegoer on a night out who collapsed in the street and suffered a serious head wound. In Warwick, a concerned member of the public calls emergency services to a suspected cardiac arrest but ends up becoming the patient himself. Plus, Neil Vann and Laura Perkins give life-saving treatment to an elderly man found unconscious face down in his hallway

programme.title: Supermarkets Unwrapped
programme.desc: The team take their trolleys down the fruit and veg aisle. Kate Quilton is in Kenya to see how summer vegetables get to British supermarkets in the thick of winter. Jimmy Doherty looks at the measures taken to transport bananas across the UK, John Whaite finds out why supermarket blackberries are so big, Helen Lawal investigates a robot berry-picker in Portugal, and Matt Tebbutt finds out how we can waste less fruit and veg

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: A firm traces heirs to the £500,000 estate of a burlesque dancer who performed at Soho's Windmill club throughout the Second World War, while another case uncovers a family's links to a comedy star's radio career. Plus, details of more unclaimed estates

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: The team heads to Birmingham, where Henry Cole stumbles upon a model metal tipper truck, which he is convinced will make some money

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The owners of Guest and the City, Dan and Mardi Leonard, welcome their visitors in Brighton, but there is a mixed reaction to the couple's contemporary decor and stained glass windows. The unconventional furniture leaves some guests confused, and one pair of fellow hoteliers bemoan the lack of sausages at breakfast

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Entrepreneur Phil Kiernan invites the contestants to the Farmer's Boy Inn in Longhope, Gloucestershire, where he challenges them to a pie-making competition. However, the task proves less entertaining than the bickering that occurs between two guests as they try to share a kitchen

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Alan and Jane Asher host on the Geanna, their purpose-built hotel boat. Dan and Mardi Leonard are concerned space is too tight, while Sarah Langford and Jean-Pierre Kujawa worry about their privacy being invaded. A trip up the Thames allows the guests to sip champagne and get to know the ropes, but the mood at dinner is subdued, and when the owners cook their first breakfast on board they find themselves bickering

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Sarah Langford and Jean-Pierre Kujawa, who have run the Sentry Mead B&B on the Isle of Wight for six years, play host in the last challenge of the week. The guests arrive with high expectations and after a few setbacks, the dinner and breakfast win everyone over

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time to learn how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers before discovering who has won the week's competition. Old tensions resurface as the results are announced, and there are heated exchanges around the table

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Swansea, performing arts director Anthony plans a theatre-themed menu for his guests, Ross, Tula, Kola and Julie. However, straight-talking Tula tries to steal the show with a candid revelation and Julie brings the house down with some frisky fun at Kola's expense. Anthony's plans go awry in the kitchen, threatening to earn him more rotten earn tomatoes than rave reviews

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Salesman Ross hosts the second party from Swansea, but his guests make a discovery that leaves him with some explaining to do, while tension erupts between him and Kola

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Social carer Kola provides plenty of hip-hop swagger on the third day of the contest in Swansea, with a Nigerian menu he hopes will both educate and impress his fellow diners

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Triathlete Tula serves a Spanish-themed menu in Swansea, hoping charm and chorizo can smooth over the dispute between Ross and Julie and avoid a punch-up over the paella

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Social worker Julie plans a chilled out slumber party for the final night of the week in Swansea. She cooks a homely menu, but struggles with the pronunciation as well as the preparation of some of her ingredients. At the end of another eventful evening, the winner of the £1,000 prize is revealed

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition kicks off in rural Co Durham at Bowlees Farm Holiday Cottages, where married couple Tony and Viv Smith hope that their tranquil setting, private hot tubs and indoor swimming pool will impress their visitors. Their guests are bowled over by the views and the facilities, but friends Jane and Rachel find fault with the cleanliness in the kitchen. At dinner, it's revealed that breakfast will be provided by the local farm shop, the only facility that Tony and Viv don't seem to have. Despite the clean plates, married couple Amanda and Howard make their feelings clear on the lack of options

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit of the week is to the Piebald Inn in Hunmanby, North Yorkshire, where the lack of a thermostat and a divisive design idea irritate some of the guests

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week takes place at Stoneleigh House Hotel, a boutique townhouse in the heart of Buckingham run by Jane Parslow and Rachel Rawding. At dinner, the guests are filled in about the Piebald's main attraction, but Jane and Viv don't see 'pie to pie' about a design idea. The group members have a mixed night's sleep, but they all agree about the quality of the breakfast - which is topped off by a surprise from John

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The fourth and final venue is glamping site Evenlode Grounds in the Cotswolds, owned by Howard and Amanda Wooliams, who impress their guests by revealing it is also a working farm

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they've been paid and to settle some scores. First up to fire back on their feedback are Tony and Viv, followed by John and Lesley, who are both desperate to know why Jane and Rachel were the only two who wouldn't return to their B&Bs

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer go house hunting in Essex and Hertfordshire. Kirstie searches for a family home for couple Justin and Christie, who have spent the first five years of their relationship living 50 miles apart, and are desperate to finally live together and start a family. Phil helps Cara and Ben search for a family home near Bishop's Stortford

programme.title: PopMaster TV
programme.desc: Ken Bruce hosts the grand final, as our music maestros are put to the ultimate test. Who will reign supreme and be crowned PopMaster TV champion 2025?

programme.title: Our Yorkshire Shop: A Victorian Restoration
programme.desc: Documentary exploring plans to restore a derelict grocer's shop in Masham, to create an elegant cafe and historic bakery, in a bid to revitalise the town. Joiner Ian Johnson makes a start by restoring the tatty shop front, discovering beautiful period windows and the shop's old 1900s awning hidden under decades of dirt

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A 13-year-old girl is rushed to St George's after being knocked off her bike at a pedestrian crossing, while in Paediatrics, a 12-year-old suffers a wound in his groin after falling from a tree and impaling his leg. programme.desc: A patient is brought in having broken his leg playing American football, while a resident at a neurological hospital is examined for signs that he has suffered a stroke. programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien head to Staffordshire to meet petrolhead Martin, who's also the organiser of a local music festival

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: A quartet of B&B owners battle it out to decide which of their establishments provides the best value for money, beginning with luxury weight-loss and detox retreat Slimmeria in East Sussex. The guests worry about uncomfortable sleeping arrangements, the price of accommodation and the strict 9pm curfew - and the prospect of a dance-style workout only exacerbates their bad mood

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Jan and William Cheeseman, owners of five-star Gold-rated 38 St Giles in Norwich, try to impress their competitors. Unfortunately, poor breakfast presentation and complaints about noise from the street increase the tension between the couple as they strive for perfection

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Ann and Bill Clay play host at their four-star Bosvean House B&B in Bude, Cornwall. Diane and Max Seivewright query the establishment's luxury description, while Galia Grainger complains about the lack of a sea view. However, the hosts hope an indulgent Cornish cream tea, a stick dance and a drink in their garden shed will succeed in winning over the guests

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Village bakers Diane and Max Seivewright, the owners of Ombersley B&B in Worcestershire, host the last round of the competition, but some guests are far from impressed with the rooms and food

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners gather to find out who has won the competition. Diane and Max Seivewright, the hosts at Ombersley B&B, feel betrayed by Ann and Bill Clay's feedback and want to know why they have been so harshly criticised, while Galia Grainger asks why Slimmeria's rules and regulations have provoked so much hostility

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The first host in Bournemouth, 38-year-old bar manager and musician Adie, hopes to hit the right notes by ensuring his night of laid back fun is full of music and laughter. With trumpets and drums at the ready and tricky crustaceans proving hard to crack, does Adie have what it takes to win the £1,000 prize?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The second Bournemouth host is aristocratic vet Sir Henry, who plans to charm his way to the prize by hosting an evening of relaxed dining

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Countryside-loving Pippa from Bournemouth hopes to impress her guests by throwing a dinner party with every course inspired by fish

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Bournemouth, spiritual student Simone hopes to wow her guests with a spicy Bajan menu. She's keen to impress her new friend, posh vet Sir Henry, but she's unsure about Pippa, who doesn't hold back as she probes Simone about her student house

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the last night in Bournemouth, flamboyant charity manager Jacqueline hopes that Michelin star-style food will win her the prize. With partridge and black pudding scotch eggs, this could be a culinary night to remember, but will Sir Henry and Simone steal her thunder with an unlikely romance?

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition kicks off at St Hilary Guest House in Llandudno, north Wales, where owner Tricia Watson and her daughter Jess hope that their classic seaside destination will impress. Guests Darren and Alex take a shine to their sink, but Debbie and Paul aren't pleased with the plumbing. At dinner, there's food for thought when some of the guests' culinary expertise is revealed, leaving the hosts in the hot seat. And there's a surprising revelation from Sue and Linda. At breakfast, cooking methods and quality are questioned, before a refusal to return has Tricia and Jess chewing over their feedback

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second of this week's destinations is The Falcon Retreat in south Wales, run by Sue Burston and her right-hand woman Linda Roberts. When the guests arrive at the secluded location, Darren and Alex are wowed by the view, but there are some issues in their room. At dinner, the guests are surprised by their hosts' previous professions, and a conversation about the additional charge for breakfast worries Sue and Linda

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week takes place at the Crown Inn in Grewelthorpe, North Yorkshire, where owners Debbie Heartfield and Paul Dixon hope to impress their guests

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final venue is Stix Glamping in Greetham, Rutland, where one guest decides glamping is not for her before she has even set foot on site, leaving her partner all alone

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Payment Day sees the B&Bers take each other to task on everything from insufficient slumber to hopeless hosting, before the payments are revealed and we find out who's won

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp searches for a family home around Hove on the south coast of England for Duncan, Anna and their three children, as they return from seven years living in Switzerland. programme.title: PopMaster TV
programme.desc: Ken Bruce hosts the penultimate episode of the series, and the last chance for players to secure a place in the grand final. Who has the best pop knowledge and will go through?

programme.title: Ancient Greece by Train with Alice Roberts
programme.desc: On the island of Paros, in the middle of the Aegean Sea, Alice joins an archaeologist on a tour of the ancient sites he has discovered in 38 years of digging. Alice takes a boat to the island of Delos where the Temple of Apollo once stood, and leaves Greece behind to go to Türkiye, where Ancient Greece was once the prevalent culture

programme.title: Castle Secrets
programme.sub-title: The Secret Escape; Tower of Torture; A King's Untamed Lust
programme.desc: A look at the fortress where a knight was trapped, while his nemesis lay patiently waiting. This edition also explores a sprawling fortress, which was the site of a dynasty's gruesome and inventive form of punishment. Plus, examining a king's palace, which was home to the shady secrets of all his marital failures

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Paramedics rush to save a grandmother with severe osteoporosis who fell down the stairs carrying her four-month-old grandson. In Stratford, paramedics Mandy Morrice and Michelle Godwin treat a racegoer on a night out who collapsed in the street and suffered a serious head wound. In Warwick, a concerned member of the public calls emergency services to a suspected cardiac arrest but ends up becoming the patient himself. Plus, Neil Vann and Laura Perkins give life-saving treatment to an elderly man found unconscious face down in his hallway

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: A woman at the airport suffers a suspected miscarriage, but the paramedics sent to her aid have trouble getting her back to hospital due to heavy snowfall. Crews also battle through the roads to treat a woman semi-conscious in police custody with collapsed veins

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A carpenter is rushed to St George's after partially severing his hand with a power saw, a farmer is kept under observation after puncturing his lung in a quad-bike accident, and a man suffers an unexpected electric shock from a heart implant that was installed after he survived a stab wound. He and his girlfriend recall the attack, and how it changed both their lives

programme.title: Heir Hunters

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Alan and Jane Asher host on the Geanna, their purpose-built hotel boat. programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.desc: A hyena pack prepares to attack a pride of lions that have driven them from their territory

programme.title: To Catch a Smuggler
programme.desc: While inspecting commercial cargo from Mexico, Laredo CBP discovers liquid meth concealed within paint buckets. The load weighs 560 kgs, and is worth $24million. In Atlanta, HSI conducts a controlled delivery of an air conditioner filled with 2 kgs of meth. Two people are arrested, and even more drugs are confiscated. In Nogales, CBP pursues a trend of female body carriers, and busts a woman carrying 107 grams of fentanyl internally

programme.title: Yukon Vet
programme.desc: The vet performs a check-up on two scrappy lynx, and Sierra takes charge on clinic day

programme.title: Secrets of the Zoo: Tampa

programme.desc: In Italy and Croatia, efforts to save a rare species of bear are helping many other animals to thrive and landscapes to flourish

programme.title: Europe's New Wild

programme.title: Border Protection Spain

programme.title: To Catch a Smuggler

programme.title: Trafficked with Mariana Van Zeller
programme.desc: Despite now being mainstream, Cryptocurrencies still lack regulation. Mariana embeds with a crew of scammers who use this to swindle millions of dollars in mere weeks

programme.desc: Cyril and the team brave perilous jungles, toothy predators and slippery volcanic rocks in order to help save French Polynesia's giant trevally

programme.title: Yukon Vet

programme.title: Plunderer: The Life and Times of a Nazi Art Thief
programme.desc: Part two of two. Jonathan Petropoulos investigates Nazi art dealer Bruno Lohse's life in post-war America. Dealing in looted art, he became an invisible hand, even as the original owners' families sought the return of works rightfully theirs. Featuring an archive interviews with Lohse himself, as well as curators, investigators and descendants of the victims of Nazi art theft

programme.title: Grand Tours of the Scottish Islands
programme.desc: Paul Murton sets off from Mallaig to explore a group of islands famous for their unusual names - Rum, Muck and Eigg. Along the way, he meets the real 'Lord Muck', visits the dramatic Kinloch Castle and joins the residents of Eigg as they celebrate their community buyout

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance for viewers to purchase goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance for viewers to purchase goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Full Steam Ahead
programme.desc: Historians Ruth Goodman, Alex Langlands and Peter Ginn continue to relive the golden age of steam and how the Victorian railways created modern Britain. They visit Beamish in County Durham, learning how the rail companies first realised there was money to be made in moving people rather than commodities. Then Peter and Alex live the life of navvies, the men who built the thousands of miles of tracks in all weather conditions, and Ruth finds out what impact the new railroads had on the cottage industries. She also learns about the role of the guard, and the trio learn what precautions were necessary for taking a long journey on a train that was yet to include the necessary facilities

programme.title: Battlefield America
programme.desc: Highlighting the strategic decisions of Ulysses S. Grant and Robert E. Lee and the intense combat that defined the crucial battles of Vicksburg and Gettysburg, underscoring their impact on the broader conflict and American history. Part two of three

programme.title: France: The Post War Recovery
programme.desc: Part one of two. The summer of 1944 marked the end of dark times for France. Aspiring to a better future, its people rolled up their sleeves and set out about rebuilding the nation's industry and agriculture. However, in post Second World War France, hopes and reality soon collided

programme.title: France: The Post War Recovery
programme.desc: Part two of two. From the Liberation of Paris to the Évian Accords signing Algeria's independence, via major social crisis of 1947, Abbé Pierre's appeal against poverty, the rise of modern comfort or the OAS attacks, this documentary paints a vast picture of a country torn between unity and division in the wake of the Second World War

programme.title: Grand Tours of the Scottish Islands
programme.desc: Paul Murton continues his island-hopping odyssey, setting sail on an island pilgrimage in the footsteps of saints, visiting Lismore, Colonsay and Oronsay. He hears the story of Saint Moluag, who established a monastery on Lismore, joins writer Alexander McCall Smith on his yacht, and uncovers the secrets revealed by a Viking ship burial on Colonsay

programme.title: Grand Tours of the Scottish Islands
programme.sub-title: Islands of the Forth: Fortress Islands of the Forth
programme.desc: Paul Murton continues his island-hopping odyssey, exploring the islands scattered in the Firth of Forth. He discovers these seemingly peaceful islands have a dramatic history of warfare, from medieval English raids on Inchcolm to the first air-raid attack on Britain above Inchgarvie. Paul learns about the strategic importance of Inchkeith's defences during two world wars before heading to the Bass Rock to learn more about the history of prisoners incarcerated on the island, with 160,000 gannets for company

programme.title: Full Steam Ahead
programme.desc: The historians reveal how the railways transformed the British diet, rescuing a nation that was struggling to feed itself. Alex Langlands and Peter Ginn discover how the mass transportation of livestock gave birth to the traditional British roast, and Ruth Goodman is in Whitby, which became a thriving fishing town thanks to the North Yorkshire Moors Railway supplying its kippers. Plus, Hampshire's watercress line, which brought nutritional salad to the masses, and how the use of steam-powered engines revolutionised production at Britain's oldest brewery

programme.title: Battlefield America
programme.desc: Showcasing the intense fighting and strategic errors in the Battle of Chickamauga that led to significant losses on both sides. Plus, a look at General Grant's Overland Campaign, a series of battles aimed at defeating General Lee's forces. Part three of three

programme.title: Constantine the Great
programme.desc: The first in a two-part profile of the ancient Roman emperor, as archaeologists plot the different stages of his life from Naissus to Rome

programme.title: Secret History
programme.desc: With access to the first nationwide survey and state-of-the-art drones mapping the Great Wall of China, this documentary offers new evidence that reveals its extraordinary magnitude, rewrites its millennia-long history and decodes its complex signals system. Using cutting-edge chemistry, the programme also finds out what has kept the wall standing for so long - a simple cooking ingredient found in kitchens across the world

programme.title: Murder on CCTV

programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell revisit the case of Murray Phillips, who was looking for his older brother Ricky. There is also an update on Ann Melbourne, who traced her birth mother 14 years ago, and Jean Smith, who was looking for her American birth father, Johnny Perkins

programme.title: The Yorkshire Auction House
programme.desc: Angus visits a house packed to the rafters with vintage copper. Then he helps a couple clear their home before they relocate to Thailand

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: In Sunderland, Claire Rawle finds out about the Tynemouth Volunteer Life Brigade, and military fan Paul Laidlaw visits an Observation Post in Blyth

programme.title: Rich House, Poor House
programme.desc: Businessman Adam and his father Ian, who live in a four-bedroom mansion in Essex, swap with single mum Kiptieu, who lives in a small council flat in Abbey Wood, London. With a number of successful businesses under his belt, Adam now travels round the world, interviewing the great and the good from showbusiness, sport and business, including the likes of John Travolta, 50 Cent and Floyd Mayweather, sharing their stories of success with clients who are willing to pay Adam thousands for the inside track. Kiptieu fled war-torn Sierra Leone and is a widow bringing up her three daughters in a two-bedroom ground-floor flat. Kiptieu also recently lost her job because of the Covid-19 pandemic, so the family budget is tighter than ever

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Actress Anna Maxwell Martin researches her family tree. Travelling to the small town in Northern Ireland where her father grew up, her understanding of her grandparents - who suffered a heartbreaking family tragedy - is turned on its head. On her mother's side, Anna starts with her grandfather Maxwell, whose name she added to her own to create her stage name. Looking into his childhood, she uncovers a dark history of domestic abuse and family separation. However, she also discovers extraordinary familial solidarity and love that changed the pattern for the generations that followed

programme.title: Celebrity Help! My House Is Haunted
programme.desc: Comedian and writer, Dom Joly, has heard eerie opera singing coming from his son's bedroom. Jayne uncovers a fascinating historical music connection

programme.title: Celebrity Help! My House Is Haunted

programme.desc: Celebrities trace their family trees and uncover facts about their heritage. In the first episode, actress Una Stubbs embarks on a quest to learn about the grandparents she never met. programme.desc: Neven Maguire ventures to Northumberland, the northernmost county in England. programme.desc: The latest national and international news

programme.title: Room to Improve
programme.desc: Architect Dermot Bannon and quantity surveyor Claire Irwin help empty nesters Deirdre and Kieran Kelly at Clonsilla in west Dublin. The couple are hoping to downsize from their spacious existing home to their beloved first home. To give Deirdre and Kieran the space they need, Dermot sets out to combine inside and outside with a triple-zoned ground floor, a glass corner extension and clever landscaping

programme.title: Maigret
programme.desc: A student is stabbed to death in the street. Maigret digs into the killer's psychology. Countess Sophie calls, fearing for her life

programme.title: Turbulence - The Story of Ryanair
programme.desc: The story of Ryanair's 40-year ascension from a single route airline that challenged the likes of Aer Lingus to a giant of European aviation, with input from insiders, commentators and former staff

programme.title: All That Remains with Niall Breslin
programme.desc: The rugby player-turned-academic and mental health advocate sets out on an emotional quest to remember more than 1,300 people buried in unmarked graves in the grounds of the former St Loman's psychiatric hospital in his native Mullingar. He uncovers stories of adults and children who were committed, died and buried under numbered metal crosses without names. Today, even those crosses have been removed. He explores the burial practices of patients unclaimed after death and investigates how many more institutional graveyards there are like this around the country - and what can be done to respect and protect them

programme.title: Masters: Keepers of Tradition
programme.desc: A look at Irish practitioners of rare, traditional and remarkable crafts and skills, starting with one of the country's finest stone carvers, Julia Gebel. She first arrived in Ireland from Germany to work on the restoration of Cormac's Chapel at the Rock of Cashel, the ancient seat of the Kings of Munster. Here she takes on a commission for a bust of Irish revolutionary Dick Barrett

programme.title: Cheap Irish Homes
programme.desc: Maggie Molloy explores more of Ireland's most reasonably priced homes, joined by building engineer Kieran McCarthy, who advises house hunters about the condition of the properties. They begin by heading to Carlow to help find a home for civil servant Laura, who wants to escape the rising costs of Dublin

programme.title: Build Your Own Home
programme.desc: Harrison Gardner is in Westmeath, helping Rose and Marguax as they renovate a stone cottage - a project that includes building a timber-framed extension - while also handling the pressure and excitement of expecting their first baby

programme.title: Hooked
programme.desc: Dr Brian Pennie lifts the lid on how modern addictions are rewiring people's brains, and asks what they can do to break free. In the first episode, Dr Pennie focuses on phones, gaming and gambling, interwoven with his personal story of going from heroin addiction to becoming a neuroscientist and lecturer at Trinity College Dublin

programme.title: Clean Sweep
programme.desc: Shelly faces mounting pressure after being identified as the mysterious woman at the crime scene. She contemplates escape, but her love for her children prevails and she decides to stay put. Crime thriller, starring Charlene McKenna

programme.title: Nationwide
programme.desc: Hearing from two men with a passion for preservation. One of them has brought an old fishing boat, which was thought lost forever, back from the brink to sail again on the Estuary of the River Suir. The other is a retired Tipperary man, who devotes much of his time to maintaining his local cemetery in Cloughprior in a rural part of North Tipperary. Richard Downes presents

programme.title: FIFA Women's World Cup Qualifier
programme.desc: France v Republic of Ireland (Kick-off 8.00pm). Marie Crowe presents coverage of both teams' concluding Group A2 match, held at Stade des Alpes in Grenoble. Captain Katie McCabe gave the Republic a shock lead in March's reverse fixture in Dublin, but a brace by Melvine Malard gave France a 2-1 win and they will be favourites to prevail once again, even though Carla Ward's side have acquitted themselves well throughout the campaign. With analysis from Karen Duggan and Louise Quinn

programme.title: The Light in the Hall

programme.title: Twenty Twenty Six
programme.desc: With the start of the tournament weeks away, final plans for the opening ceremony need to be pinned down. But its Mexican creative director has had a rethink, and his new vision doesn't include any reference to America at all, or indeed to soccer. The lead events producer may or may not be close to a having a breakdown. Also, Ian finds himself starting to think about life after football

programme.title: Football's Financial Shame: The Story of the V-11

programme.title: Twitter: Breaking the Bird
programme.desc: The inside story of the tech entrepreneurs who created the social media app Twitter. At first the site grew on the back of celebrities who realised it offered them a direct way to communicate with fans. After going global, it seemed to be fulfilling the founders' dream of a digital utopia where all voices would be heard. But as hate speech and misinformation flooded the platform, the founders faced growing problems to control it - and the sale to Elon Musk in 2022 represented the end of their dream

programme.desc: Siân Walters and James Payne explore Leonardo da Vinci's The Last Supper, a painting that is lauded for its technical brilliance thanks to the artist's use of mathematics, perspective and composition

programme.title: Ponies
programme.desc: Twila runs her first asset Vera, a Russian fixer with secrets tied to her husband's death

programme.title: The Gilded Age
programme.desc: Peggy gets welcomed back to 61st Street by almost everyone, while George informs Oscar of his decision and sends Clay to meet with union leader Mr Henderson at his steel mill

programme.desc: The opera war escalates, there is a surprise guest at Bertha's fundraiser, and Peggy offers to cover the Tuskegee story. Ada hosts a luncheon for Mr Forte, George woos Henderson and Watson mulls over a proposal

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street
programme.desc: Bayliss gets a surprise which could alter his holiday plans, while Ballard and Gharty investigate the death of an HIV positive man and Munch is at loggerheads with his ex-wife. Cop drama, starring Yaphet Kotto and Peter Gerety

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street
programme.desc: Lewis and Falsone investigate the baffling case of a man found lynched, while Pembleton is puzzled to see Bayliss flirting with Detective Ballard

programme.title: Urban Secrets
programme.desc: Alan Cumming tours the less-celebrated sights of Brighton, beginning by visiting the Toy and Model Museum to look at its collection of model trains. He then discovers a boutique that sells a variety of unusual items, before taking a glimpse behind the scenes at the Royal Pavilion

programme.title: Fish Town
programme.desc: The cameras follow three teams of fishermen participating in Brixham's annual Trawler Race, including a father and son who have been pitted against each other. Landlady Pat Seddon is not looking forward to the raucous celebrations, however, and decides to reminisce about past race days instead. The programme also meets Matt Kimble, a graduate who gave up on his plan to become a PE teacher and decided to follow his ancestors into the fishing trade instead

programme.desc: While in Los Angeles to sell her new set as a comedy special, Deborah pitches a role to a former collaborator, and Ava experiences a number of fortuitous run-ins

programme.title: Hacks
programme.desc: After securing an offer from her ideal venue, Deborah prepares to take the stage for her taping. Jimmy learns his clients are being poached by a formidable foe, while Ava deals with a new opportunity.

programme.title: The Gilded Age
programme.desc: Bertha begins to rethink her loyalty to the Metropolitan Opera, an engagement party leaves Marian full of doubts, and George strikes a deal with union leader Bill Henderson

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street
programme.desc: Part two of two. Another priest is murdered after the two Guatemalan political refugees suspected of killing the first one are set free

programme.title: Urban Secrets
programme.desc: Alan Cumming explores Newcastle, discovering strange sounds by the river and finding out how traditional culture is being reinterpreted in the Byker district. He goes behind the scenes at Newcastle United with local football hero Peter Beardsley, learns about the history of adult comic Viz from former editor Simon Donald and accompanies comedienne Sarah Millican to her favourite part of the city

programme.title: Mulholland Drive (2001)
programme.desc: An actress arrives home to find a mysterious woman in her room who is suffering from amnesia. She sets out with the stranger to discover how she got there - but the trail quickly leads to intrigue, blackmail and murder. David Lynch's mystery, starring Naomi Watts, Justin Theroux, Laura Harring and Robert Forster

programme.title: Mad Max (1979)
programme.desc: In the near future, Australian cop Max Rockatansky tires of battling to impose law and order on the roads and turns in his badge - only to be forced out of retirement when his wife and child are murdered at the hands of a motorcycle gang. George Miller's action thriller, starring Mel Gibson, Joanne Samuel and Hugh Keays-Byrne

programme.title: The Equalizer (2014)
programme.desc: A retired secret agent applies his talents to the task of helping victims of injustice. When he witnesses a prostitute being beaten up by a pimp, he kills the man, an act that draws him into a battle to the death with a Russian crime syndicate. Action thriller based on the 1980s TV series, starring Denzel Washington and Chloe Grace Moretz

programme.title: Ferrari (2023)
programme.desc: During the summer of 1957, ex-racecar driver Enzo Ferrari is in crisis. Bankruptcy stalks the company he and his wife, Laura, built from nothing a decade earlier. Their tempestuous marriage struggles with the mourning for one son and the acknowledgement of another. Attempting to secure their survival, he rolls the dice on one race - 1,000 miles across Italy, the iconic Mille Miglia. Director Michael Mann's drama, starring Adam Driver and Jack O'Connell

programme.title: Peggy Sue Got Married (1986)
programme.desc: A depressed fortysomething woman about to get divorced faints at a high-school reunion and awakes to find she has travelled back in time to her teenage years. Given the chance to put things right, she resolves to avoid a relationship with the man she is about to divorce. Comedy, starring Kathleen Turner, Nicolas Cage, Maureen O'Sullivan, Joan Allen, Catherine Hicks and Kevin J O'Connor

programme.title: Lost in Translation (2003)
programme.desc: A lonely and past-his-prime American actor travels to Japan to film a commercial. During a depressing night in the hotel bar, he meets a spirited younger woman and the pair strike up a close friendship, exploring Tokyo and helping each other face the mundanities of everyday life. Sofia Coppola's comedy drama, starring Bill Murray, Scarlett Johansson and Giovanni Ribisi

programme.title: A Big Bold Beautiful Journey (2025)
programme.desc: New York bachelor David is due to drive to his friend's wedding, but a wheel clamp on his vehicle derails those plans. He acquires a new drive from the mysterious Car Rental Company managed by two nameless agents, who are oddly familiar with his dating history. At the wedding, David meets fellow singleton Sarah and squanders an opportunity to dance with her. On the way home, the rental car's GPS asks him if he would like to embark on a big bold beautiful journey. When David agrees, the virtual assistant guides him to Sarah and they follow a winding route between physical doors that open into episodes from their respective pasts. Romantic drama, starring Colin Farrell and Margot Robbie

programme.title: Materialists (2025)
programme.desc: Failed actress Lucy Mason is a star employee of the Adore matchmaking agency. She prefers the life of a singleton after her previous relationship with actor John imploded because of his precarious finances. At a client's wedding, Lucy meets the groom's brother, Harry, and politely rebuffs his amorous advances, suggesting he should enrol with Adore. Harry persists and Lucy agrees to go on a date, on condition that he signs up with the agency after their lunchtime liaison. Writer/director Celine Song's romantic comedy, starring Dakota Johnson, Chris Evans and Pedro Pascal

programme.title: Dog Day Afternoon (1975)
programme.desc: Hapless Sonny and his sidekick Sal have a plan to rob a Brooklyn bank. The novice crooks thought they would be in and out in 10 minutes, but instead find themselves stuck in an ongoing hostage situation, with the police and the media laying siege to the bank. Sidney Lumet's fact-based drama, starring Al Pacino, John Cazale and Charles Durning.

programme.title: Full Metal Jacket (1987)
programme.desc: Raw recruits undergo harsh training under a domineering Marine drill sergeant after being called up for service in the Vietnam War, but his brutal methods lead to the breakdown of one of his cadets. Eventually, some of the fully trained soldiers get their first taste of combat in 1968. Stanley Kubrick's drama, starring Matthew Modine, Adam Baldwin, Vincent D'Onofrio and R Lee Ermey

programme.title: The Shining (1980)
programme.desc: A writer takes a caretaking job at a remote mountain hotel. It has closed for the winter, and he has been hired to get the place ready for the next season. But evil spirits haunting its corridors destroy his sanity, causing the unhinged author to rampage through the hotel and terrorise his wife and young son. Stanley Kubrick's horror, based on the Stephen King novel, with Jack Nicholson, Shelley Duvall, Danny Lloyd and Scatman Crothers

programme.title: A Clockwork Orange (1971)
programme.desc: Sadistic young gang leader Alex leads a carefree life indulging his love of rape, violence and classical music. When he is finally arrested, he is subjected to a sinister form of aversion therapy that brainwashes him into being physically unable to use violence. However, on his release, he finds friends and family have turned on him, while others seek to exploit him to further their political ends. Drama, starring Malcolm McDowell and Warren Clarke

programme.title: Mad Max 2 (1981)
programme.desc: A former cop haunted by his tragic past roams the wasteland of Australia in the aftermath of a nuclear war. He arrives at a besieged settlement and grudgingly helps protect the residents from a horde of marauding bikers who want to get hold of their valuable supply of petrol. Futuristic adventure sequel, starring Mel Gibson, Bruce Spence, Vernon Wells, Emil Minty and Kjell Nilsson

programme.title: Peggy Sue Got Married (1986)
programme.desc: A depressed fortysomething woman about to get divorced faints at a high-school reunion and awakes to find she has travelled back in time to her teenage years. Given the chance to put things right, she resolves to avoid a relationship with the man she is about to divorce. Comedy, starring Kathleen Turner, Nicolas Cage, Maureen O'Sullivan, Joan Allen, Catherine Hicks and Kevin J O'Connor

programme.title: Detroit (2017)
programme.desc: A group of rogue police officers responds to a complaint with apparent retribution rather than justice in mind, launching a raid on a group of African-Americans. Public anger reaches a peak after the incident, as relationships sour with authority. Fact-based drama set during the 1967 Detroit riots, starring John Boyega, Anthony Mackie and Algee Smith

programme.title: Full Metal Jacket (1987)
programme.desc: Raw recruits undergo harsh training under a domineering Marine drill sergeant after being called up for service in the Vietnam War, but his brutal methods lead to the breakdown of one of his cadets. Eventually, some of the fully trained soldiers get their first taste of combat in 1968. Stanley Kubrick's drama, starring Matthew Modine, Adam Baldwin, Vincent D'Onofrio and R Lee Ermey

programme.title: The Shining (1980)
programme.desc: A writer takes a caretaking job at a remote mountain hotel. It has closed for the winter, and he has been hired to get the place ready for the next season. But evil spirits haunting its corridors destroy his sanity, causing the unhinged author to rampage through the hotel and terrorise his wife and young son. Stanley Kubrick's horror, based on the Stephen King novel, with Jack Nicholson, Shelley Duvall, Danny Lloyd and Scatman Crothers

programme.title: Mulholland Drive (2001)
programme.desc: An actress arrives home to find a mysterious woman in her room who is suffering from amnesia. She sets out with the stranger to discover how she got there - but the trail quickly leads to intrigue, blackmail and murder. David Lynch's mystery, starring Naomi Watts, Justin Theroux, Laura Harring and Robert Forster

programme.title: Black Bag (2025)
programme.desc: When a spy is suspected of treason, her husband faces the ultimate test - loyalty to his marriage or his country. Director Steven Soderbergh's thriller, starring Michael Fassbender, Cate Blanchett and Rege-Jean Page

programme.title: The Fifth Element (1997)
programme.desc: In the 23rd century, the world faces destruction from a seemingly unstoppable force of pure evil. Meanwhile, a New York cab driver encounters a mysterious woman destined to save the Earth from its fate, and has to protect her from a scheming arms dealer and a horde of alien mercenaries. Luc Besson's sci-fi adventure, starring Bruce Willis, Milla Jovovich and Gary Oldman

programme.title: Arrival (2016)
programme.desc: The world is thrown into uproar when 12 alien spaceships arrive at locations around the globe. As tensions rise between nations and fear of the arrivals grows, a recently bereaved linguist is recruited by the US army to find a way to communicate with the aliens and discover why they have come. Sci-fi drama, starring Amy Adams, Jeremy Renner and Forest Whitaker

programme.title: Gravity (2013)
programme.desc: Two astronauts are left stranded in orbit above the Earth when the debris from an exploding satellite destroys their shuttle. Left drifting in space with no way to contact mission control, they begin a perilous journey to reach the safety of a space station before their oxygen supply runs out. Thriller, starring Sandra Bullock and George Clooney

programme.title: Paycheck (2003)
programme.desc: An engineer carries out a top-secret job on the understanding that his knowledge of the task will be erased from his mind afterwards to protect his employer's intellectual property. However, waking after the memory-wiping procedure, he is shocked to learn he had somehow agreed to waive the huge payment due to him - and is wanted for a murder he does not remember committing. John Woo's thriller, starring Ben Affleck, Uma Thurman and Aaron Eckhart

programme.title: American Gangster (2007)
programme.desc: The story of 1970s drug kingpin Frank Lucas, whose innovative methods of smuggling heroin into the US made him one of the leading figures in the New York underworld. A detective is assigned to bring Lucas's empire down, but his investigation is undermined by police corruption. Ridley Scott's fact-based crime drama, starring Denzel Washington, Russell Crowe, Chiwetel Ejiofor and Josh Brolin

programme.title: Mad Max Beyond Thunderdome (1985)
programme.desc: The nomadic warrior pitches up in the city of a cut-throat desert community in search of his stolen vehicle and ends up making a deal with a local dictator to get rid of her rival for power. However, when the bargain turns sour, he is cast out into the wilderness - where he becomes the unlikely protector of a tribe of feral youngsters. Sci-fi adventure sequel, starring Mel Gibson, Tina Turner and Bruce Spence

programme.title: Seven Psychopaths (2012)
programme.desc: A writer struggling to complete the script for a movie about psychopathic killers turns to his criminal friends for advice. He ends up getting more than he bargained for when the small-time crooks kidnap a dog that belongs to a gangster, putting all their lives in danger. Comedy thriller, starring Colin Farrell, Sam Rockwell, Christopher Walken and Woody Harrelson

programme.title: Black Bag (2025)
programme.desc: When a spy is suspected of treason, her husband faces the ultimate test - loyalty to his marriage or his country. Director Steven Soderbergh's thriller, starring Michael Fassbender, Cate Blanchett and Rege-Jean Page

programme.title: Him
programme.desc: Cameron Cade is predicted to be the next great quarterback of American football. During a night-time training session, he suffers a traumatic brain injury. Cameron agrees to attend a week-long boot camp run by retiring star quarterback Isaiah White at his secluded desert compound. However, Cade's life soon turns into a nightmare. Supernatural horror, starring Marlon Wayans and Tyriq Withers

programme.title: The Plague (2025)
programme.desc: A socially awkward tween endures the ruthless hierarchy at a water polo camp as his anxiety spirals into psychological turmoil over the summer. Thriller, starring Joel Edgerton and Everett Blunck

programme.title: We Bury the Dead (2024)
programme.desc: When an experimental military weapon accidentally devastates Tasmania, volunteers travel to the island to help authorities identify the dead. Among them is a Ava Newman, who secretly hopes to find her missing husband. However, some of the bodies seem to be returning to life. Horror, starring Daisy Ridley, Brenton Thwaites and Mark Coles Smith

programme.title: Tafiti - Across The Desert (2025)
programme.desc: A young meerkat's grandpa is bitten by a snake, so he sets off to find a magical flower for the cure. Animated adventure, featuring the voices of Cosima Henman, Steve Hudson and Kathleen Renish

programme.title: The Internship (2026)
programme.desc: A CIA-trained assassin recruits other graduates from her secret childhood programme, the Internship, to violently destroy the organisation. Thriller, starring Lizzy Greene, Megan Boone and Sky Katz

programme.title: Fuze (2026)
programme.desc: An unexploded Second World War bomb is discovered on a busy construction site in the centre of London. Chaos ensues as the military and police begin a mass evacuation. Drama, starring Aaron Taylor-Johnson, Theo James, Gugu Mbatha-Raw and Sam Worthington

programme.title: The Plague (2025)
programme.desc: A socially awkward tween endures the ruthless hierarchy at a water polo camp as his anxiety spirals into psychological turmoil over the summer. Thriller, starring Joel Edgerton and Everett Blunck

programme.title: Dead Man's Wire (2026)
programme.desc: Tony Kiritsis is convinced mortgage broker ML Hall cheated him out of profits from a land development deal and he vows revenge. Tony walks into a branch of the Meridian Mortgage company for a meeting with ML, only to find his target is on holiday and the appointment will be with ML's son Richard instead. Regardless, Tony reveals a shotgun and holds Richard hostage for his father's supposed crimes. Director Gus Van Sant's thriller, starring Bill Skarsgård, Al Pacino and Dacre Montgomery

programme.title: Sisu: Road To Revenge (2025)
programme.desc: During the Second World War, Finland reluctantly cedes territory to the invading Red Army. Former Army commando Aatami Korpi is among the affected and returns to his family plot to deconstruct his timber-framed home and relocate the wooden beams on a truck across the new border separating the Soviet Union and Finland. The legend of the 'the man who refuses to die' remains a thorn in the side of the Soviet army, and a nameless KGB officer agrees to liberate high-ranking brute Igor Draganov from a Siberian prison in exchange for killing Aatami. Finnish action sequel, starring Jorma Tommila, Stephen Lang and Richard Brake

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the unsolved murder of Crimewatch presenter and newsreader Jill Dando, who was shot dead outside her house in Fulham, west London, in April 1999. Intense media coverage followed and several lines of enquiry were pursued, including a theory that she had been killed by a jealous ex-boyfriend and the idea that somebody had hired a hitman in revenge for a conviction linked to Crimewatch. Following a trial at the Old Bailey, 40-year-old Barry George was found guilty of Dando's murder, but a third appeal quashed his original conviction and resulted in his acquittal in 2009

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts spends a year visiting archaeological digs around the country, finding out about discoveries that shed new light on British history. In Buckinghamshire, Simon Mays and Jill Eyers use a recent rediscovery of artefacts from a 1912 excavation to investigate what appears to have been a mass infanticide during the Roman era. Alice also meets the team examining one of the largest discoveries of Roman coins ever made in Britain, and hears about a mystery in Dorset

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts visits Happisburgh in Norfolk, where the discovery of rare artefacts provides evidence of the earliest human occupation in Britain, challenging the conventional belief that mankind would not have been able to survive in northern Europe one million years ago. She also joins a team at Forteviot in Scotland, where the uncovering of a Bronze Age grave holds a big surprise

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts travels to Bamburgh, Northumberland, to learn about the generations of Anglo Saxons who lived and worked there, gaining an insight into the illnesses they suffered and the societies in which they lived. She visits the Sutton Hoo site, as well as a pioneering project in Sittingbourne, Kent, which was set up following the unearthing of an Anglo Saxon cemetery, and looks at the Staffordshire hoard discovered by a metal detector in 2009

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain
programme.desc: In 1996, two girls and their mother enjoying a walk in the Kent countryside were the victims of a frenzied hammer attack by Michael Stone, who left them for dead. Lin Russell and her six-year-old daughter Megan died from their injuries, but nine-year-old Josie survived and made a remarkable recovery. This film examines the events surrounding the murders and features interviews with Shaun Russell - Lin's widower and the girls' father - Josie's close friend Lucy Gregson, her former primary school teacher Lynda Roberts and police family liaison officer Pauline Smith

programme.title: Apocalypse D-Day
programme.desc: Part one of two. The story of the key turning point of the Second World War, which started on June 6, 1944. A titanic fleet took on the beaches of Normandy, intent on liberating Europe from Hitler's Nazis. Thanks to the lessons learned from the Dieppe raid of August 1942 and supported by the Provence landing of August 1944, the mission was a spectacular success as the Allies succeeded in moving beyond their political differences, overcoming logistical difficulties and carrying out an operation with unprecedented military stakes

programme.desc: An examination of the 100 days it took for the Allies to liberate Normandy, to break through the German line and to pave the way towards victory. programme.desc: Experts investigate why so many UFOs have been spotted near remote islands, and consider whether the remote outposts could be the earthly homes of aliens

programme.title: Combat Ships
programme.desc: Stories of hospital ships and those who served on them, including the tragic story of the sinking of the Titanic's sister vessel, The Britannic

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain
programme.desc: The case of Jeremy Bamber, who was sentenced to life in prison for murdering his family in 1985, but decades later still maintains his innocence

programme.title: The Abyss: Rise & Fall of the Nazis
programme.desc: The invasion of Poland by the German army in 1939, examining how Hitler succeeded in pushing through his rearmament and war plans relatively unhindered

programme.title: History's Greatest Myths
programme.desc: The themes of society and myths that engulf memories of the past. These myths often become woven into the fabric of society and can pass through generations. Myths surrounding feminism and the suffragettes still swirl, while links between these myths can also be seen through the stories of the Salem Witch Trials. Suffering is a recurring theme throughout the ages and this is explored in the myths of the bloody murders of Jack the Ripper and suffering as a result of the Stock Market crash

programme.title: Pharaohs at War
programme.sub-title: Ramses II - The Battle of Kadesh
programme.desc: The Battle of Kadesh, fought in 1275 BCE between the Egyptian Empire led by pharaoh Ramesses II and the Hittite Empire led by king Muwatalli II. In one of the world's largest chariot battles, fought beside the Orontes River, Ramses sought to wrest Canaan and Syria from the Hittites and recapture the city of Kadesh. There was a day of carnage as approximately 5,000 chariots charged into the fray, but no outright victor. Ultimately, the battle led to the world's first recorded peace treaty

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Experts investigate an Italian mountain that has stood witness to inexplicable phenomena for centuries, wondering whether it could somehow be attracting alien visitors

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Experts question the link between divine substances and extraterrestrials, wondering if the 'food of the gods' really has been sustaining Earth's alien visitors

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain
programme.desc: The case of Jeremy Bamber, who was sentenced to life in prison for murdering his family in 1985, but decades later still maintains his innocence

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: The fearsome and mysterious gods worshipped by the Norse Vikings, considering whether the super-beings that guided the Vikings were actually visitors from another planet

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: The movement known as Ancient Astronaut Theory, which was sparked by Erich von Daniken, Swiss author of several books which make controversial claims about extraterrestrial influences on early human culture

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Ancient emperors, kings and pharaohs were often attributed with otherworldly strengths, abilities and wisdom, and many were even considered gods. This programme considers whether ancient rulers had otherworldly links. Experts examine whether history's most powerful leaders were 'chosen' and guided by aliens

programme.title: Canadian Pickers
programme.desc: A man is ready to liquidate his collection of dinosaur fossils and other quirky items and Scott hunts for maritime treasures while Sheldon eats oysters

programme.title: Commando Missions
programme.desc: Operation Frankton, of December 1942. The mission was for Royal Marines to place limpet mines on German ships in the occupied Bordeaux Harbour, 62 miles along the Gironde River. Five cockleshell canoes set off from submarine HMS Tuna at the mouth of the river. Only two reached the harbour and carried out the raid, escaping with the aid of the French Resistance. The damage to the ships and the resulting disruption to German supply lines had a significant impact on the war effort

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts spends a year visiting archaeological digs around the country, finding out about discoveries that shed new light on British history. In a Roman-themed first edition, she examines the mystery surrounding the skeletons of 97 babies found beside the Thames, a newly discovered town in rural Devon and a cult figure buried for 1,700 years beneath a fort

programme.title: World War II with Tom Hanks
programme.desc: After Pearl Harbor Japanese forces sweep through Southeast Asia. On Guadalcanal, US Marines face a brutal fight to halt their relentless advance now

programme.title: World War II with Tom Hanks
programme.desc: As Hitler's power spreads across Europe, his hateful ideology drives persecution of Jewish people, leading to escalating violence and ultimately the Holocaust

programme.title: Secrets Declassified with David Duchovny
programme.desc: The actor steps inside secret spaces where history was shaped, from Churchill's bunker to hidden bases built to protect power in times of conflict

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.desc: The Lord of Lancaster asserts his reign by levying tariffs on the 'little people'. Lisa and Kerri stitch together some antique accoutrements. Dusty and Lupe spot some gold in an immense locker, while Kenny finally finds himself some decent profit

programme.title: Forged with Steele
programme.desc: Blade expert Alec Steele heads to Montana for another season of historic weapon-crafting. This time, the iconic Viking sword and Japanese throwing star are just a few of the recreations

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts finds out about discoveries that shed light on the Tudor Age, visiting excavations at Shakespeare's first theatre in London's Shoreditch and at his last home in Stratford-upon-Avon, where clues reveal his economical use of money. In Wales, she meets a team of archaeologists learning about the realities of Henry VIII's dissolution of the monasteries, while the history of a forgotten royal palace is uncovered on the banks of the Thames

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts spends a year visiting archaeological digs around the country, finding out about discoveries that shed new light on British history. In a Roman-themed first edition, she examines the mystery surrounding the skeletons of 97 babies found beside the Thames, a newly discovered town in rural Devon and a cult figure buried for 1,700 years beneath a fort

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts explores archaeological digs at sites dating back to the Viking age, offering an insight into how the seafaring warriors from Scandinavia lived across Britain. In the Outer Hebrides, she examines signs of the earliest Norse settlers, before inspecting a collection of silver and gold pieces discovered in York. There is also a look at some of the most treasured Viking finds, including 12th-century ivory chess figures the Lewis Chessmen

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: Corey becomes enchanted with a collection of magical memorabilia. A rare first edition copy of Anne of Green Gables is offered, while a vintage Harley t-shirt makes Corey go hog wild. Plus, Rick and Chum head to California to take a peek at a massive Disney collection

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.desc: Lisa hustles on a calm Long Beach day. Ivy and Pops make a vacation stop and outdo other buyers. Dave starts strong but gets playful. Brandi and Cameron nearly reach peak profits

programme.sub-title: I'm in a Canyon Country State of Mind

programme.title: Lost U-Boats of WWII
programme.desc: After finding Nazi artifacts at a sunken pier, treasure hunter Darrell Miklos and his team continue their search for a site known as Target 3 where they find a possible component of a Nazi secret weapon that changes the direction of their investigation

programme.desc: The greatest machines, most impactful weapons and groundbreaking innovations of the Second World War, and how these technologies continue to shape the world

programme.title: Commando Missions
programme.desc: The Heraklion Operation, carried out in June 1942. Major Georges Bergé's team landed in Crete and moved across land, intending destroy Luftwaffe planes at Heraklion airfield. They destroyed about 20 Ju 88s, but despite escaping from the airfield found their retreat betrayed

programme.title: Commando Missions
programme.desc: Musketoon Operation, a daring, small-scale commando raid on a German-held electricity generating station at Glomfjord in Norway, carried out by British and Norwegian commandos in September 1942. The mission involved a submarine crossing, an overland route and the destruction of the power plant

programme.title: Commando Missions

programme.desc: Mr Burns is given six weeks to live, and the town erupts into a party mood. Shocked by their reaction, he decides to take his own life and jumps off a cliff, but fate intervenes and he suffers from a bout of amnesia
