programme.title: Pete Wicks: For Dogs' Sake
programme.sub-title: Terri, Esme and the Chi Family
programme.desc: Pete meets Lurcher Terri, who's terrified of the world and must learn to trust again, and helps settle a family of chihuahuas given up by their heartbroken owner

programme.title: Shades of Blue
programme.desc: As the FBI put pressure on Harlee, she refuses to provide any incriminating evidence on Matt - but she does mention teammate Tess's suspicious role in a murder investigation

programme.title: Shades of Blue
programme.sub-title: Who Can Tell Me Who Am I?
programme.desc: Wozniak realises Donny has been withholding information, while Harlee's attempt to feign co-operation with FBI handler Stahl backfires when she leads him to incriminating evidence. Loman crumbles under the guilt of shooting an unarmed suspect

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Consumer journalists Nikki Fox and Matt Allwright hunt for antiques in Kent alongside expert guides Natasha Raskin Sharp and David Harper. As they seek out items to sell at auction, the rookie antiques dealers find the time to learn about William Morris's manifesto to save ancient buildings, and discover the connection between Kentish ragstone and the early Victorian dinosaur hunters

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Choirmaster Gareth Malone and saxophonist YolanDa Brown hunt for items in Edinburgh and Fife, with experts David Harper and Raj Bisram on hand to advise. Among the items of note are novelty sugar tongs, a Native American drum and a rare green pig

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous
programme.desc: Eddie and Patsy take a weekend hunting trip in the country. Back home, Saffy has a baby shower with her friends, but one of them develops an unhealthy fixation. Comedy, starring Jennifer Saunders and Joanna Lumley, with guest appearances by Emma Bunton and Clarissa Dickson Wright

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous
programme.desc: Edina finds herself on the horns of a dilemma - Patsy is presenting the fashion event of the year at the Royal Albert Hall, but Saffy has gone unexpectedly into labour, leaving her mother torn between love and duty

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous
programme.desc: A new boyfriend boasting a selection of lost Beatles recordings makes Eddie think she's hit the big time, but her plans to marry him and invite an elite group of producers to hear the tapes are dashed in spectacular fashion. Elton John and Robert Lindsay guest star

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Dave Mathewson gets wind of a possible free lunch at the officers' mess when he heads off to collect a stylish Swedish Volvo P1800S from an RAF base in the Midlands

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Derek heads to Suffolk to collect two Bentleys with a majestic pedigree; Cynthia a stunning 1963 S3 and Harry, a 1965 Continental

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien search for more items to fix up and sell on, visiting a Norman castle, restoring a Fiat 500 and making a table from part of Southend pier

programme.title: Speed with Guy Martin
programme.desc: The motorcycle racer and lorry mechanic attempts, with the aid of slipstreaming, to break the British record for outright speed on a bicycle - 110mph. Guy recruits a team comprising a truck racer, a bike builder and a design engineer to prepare for the feat, and undergoes a training regime with double Olympic cycling gold medallist Laura Trott before heading to Pendine Sands on the shores of Carmarthen Bay for his challenge

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Speed with Guy Martin
programme.desc: Speed freak Guy Martin embarks on a mission to fly using muscle power alone, by building the world's fastest human-powered aircraft. He heads to Southampton University where, in 1961, Derek Piggot became the first man to fly under his own power. Guy teams up with a group of engineers from the same university to build a plane that Guy will ride like a bicycle - will he manage to fly into the record books?

programme.title: Speed with Guy Martin
programme.desc: The racing fan sets out to break the record for the world's fastest gravity-powered sled, taking expert advice from Winter Olympic gold medallist Amy Williams. Guy experiences his first taste of going blisteringly fast on ice at the famous Cresta Run in the Swiss ski resort of St Moritz, before a crack team of engineers helps him build a prototype toboggan. Then it's on to Andorra in the Pyrenees, where he hopes to steer himself into the record books on the new speed ski slope Pista Riberal

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Dave heads to Bolton to pick up a classic Italian sports car - a Lancia Fulvia 1.3 Sport styled by Milan coachbuilder Zagato

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Derek rubs shoulders with pop music royalty when he picks up a Morgan sports car, while a Second World War ambulance arrives at Thornton le Dale

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The months following prime minister Neville Chamberlain's declaration of war against Germany, in which the only casualties on the home front were those who had to endure the blackouts. Elsewhere in Europe, the Nazi war machine cut a swathe of destruction. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: Secret Nazi Bases
programme.sub-title: Dag Bromberg: A City in the Forest
programme.desc: As the Red Army approached a forest in Poland toward the end of the war, they stumbled upon a massive complex containing more than a thousand structures across 25sqkm

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and her team of experts travel to Beverley Minster in East Yorkshire, where the items up for valuation include a pair of Victorian paintings that were given as payment for a gambling debt and a medieval ring dug up on a farm. They also reveal the surprisingly eventful past of a car rescued from a pig farm

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The team pays a second visit to Beverley Minster, East Yorkshire, where the objects up for scrutiny include a pair of medical leech jars, a ring containing a highly valuable lock of hair, and a distinctive sideboard featuring some very well-hidden drawers. Presented by Fiona Bruce

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Derek Mathewson picks up a lovingly restored 1925 Calthorpe motorbike that he thinks is more of a work of art than anything ever produced by Picasso

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Dave Mathewson gets wind of a possible free lunch at the officers' mess when he heads off to collect a stylish Swedish Volvo P1800S from an RAF base in the Midlands

programme.desc: It's Robbie Cumming's toughest trip yet as he battles through water weed on the Bradley Arm and gets stuck in silt on the Ridgacre Branch

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Paul hits the road to collect an old navy ambulance that served its time as a holiday camper

programme.title: Bangers & Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The team buy a confusing 1984 French Matra Murena and send it to Citroen specialists. Next, they take on a Bond classic - no, not that one - a 1977 Arctic Wetbike!

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Derek Mathewson picks up a lovingly restored 1925 Calthorpe motorbike that he thinks is more of a work of art than anything ever produced by Picasso

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: 100 motorbikes come in for auction from every era of the 20th century, and the team assess a 1965 Vanden Plas Princess with a four litre Rolls Royce engine that's seen better days

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Dave heads to Bolton to pick up a classic Italian sports car - a Lancia Fulvia 1.3 Sport styled by Milan coachbuilder Zagato

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole restores two quad bikes and a stylish sun lounger, while Simon O'Brien rescues a holed canoe and turns an old oak beam into a spice rack

programme.title: Speed with Guy Martin
programme.desc: Speed freak Guy Martin embarks on a mission to fly using muscle power alone, by building the world's fastest human-powered aircraft. He heads to Southampton University where, in 1961, Derek Piggot became the first man to fly under his own power. Guy teams up with a group of engineers from the same university to build a plane that Guy will ride like a bicycle - will he manage to fly into the record books?

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Speed with Guy Martin
programme.desc: The motorbike racer takes part in four more speed-based challenges and gets his hands dirty in unique engineering projects to discover what makes things go fast. In the first programme, he finds out how far it's possible to travel on a bicycle during 24 hours of non-stop pedalling. With the help of bike experts Miles Kingsbury and Mike Burrows he builds a tandem, and pairs up with his friend, endurance expert Jason Miles, to see how far they can push their bodies. In the build-up to the final record attempt at the historic Goodwood Motor Circuit, Guy also learns about the science of cooking for athletes with nutritionist Nigel Mitchell, who instructs him to lose five kilos and recommends a new diet regimen

programme.title: Speed with Guy Martin
programme.desc: The motorcycle racer heads to Colorado where he hopes to win one of America's oldest races - the Pikes Peak International Hill Climb. In this annual contest, known as the Race to the Clouds, riders compete against the clock on a gruelling mountainside track that finishes more than 13,000ft above sea level. To learn the skills required in hill-climb events, Guy visits the birthplace of the sport - Shelsley Walsh in Worcestershire. Racing up the course in an F1-style car, he realises sheer horsepower and driving precision are vital, but he must also take a high-altitude fitness test in a hypoxic chamber to prepare for the challenge ahead

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: The Mathewsons take in a 1982 Audi Quattro and a Sunbeam Alpine. Plus, Derek takes delivery of a beautiful green Renault van, previously used as a bread van in Paris

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Derek heads to Scotland in the mother of all rainstorms to pick up a Jaguar E-type and a rare MGC convertible. Plus, Dave extracts a rare Austin Gipsey from a lock up

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: How, in 1940, the French Army suffered a heavy defeat after underestimating the Nazis' strength while relying on First World War strategies and equipment to defend its frontiers. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: Secret Nazi Bases
programme.desc: A secret weapons program in the heart of Austria that could have ended badly for the Allies - and may have indicated nuclear plans being concocted by Hitler

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The team heads to Somerleyton Hall, near Lowestoft, where the objects up for valuation include a bracelet given to Queen Victoria and a picture painted by a suffragette artist. The experts also investigate the origins of an intriguing box of paints that is said to have belonged to John Constable

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team visit the Steam Museum in Swindon, where objects examined by the experts include one of the rarest pieces of silver seen on the show and a valuable painting that was about to be thrown away. Among the more bizarre sights are uneaten slices of royal wedding cakes dating back to Queen Victoria, and hundreds of rail tickets for axed stations

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Derek Mathewson has sold everything from trucks to bicycles - but he can't remember the last time he auctioned off a two-seater plane

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: 100 motorbikes come in for auction from every era of the 20th century, and the team assess a 1965 Vanden Plas Princess with a four litre Rolls Royce engine that's seen better days

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming encounters a leaky lock pound on the Perry Barr Flight and gets marooned in the mud before travelling under Spaghetti Junction in Birmingham

programme.title: Train Truckers
programme.desc: The A4 LNER Pacific Locomotive, Sir Nigel Gresley, must be hauled 46 miles at 30mph from Crewe to Bridgnorth

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough turns his attention to life in Asia, the planet's most varied and extreme continent, which stretches from the Arctic Circle to the equator. Walruses gather in huge numbers in the frozen north and brown bears roam around remote Russian volcanoes, while the deep jungle provides sanctuary for the last few Sumatran rhino. There is also a glimpse of rarely seen creatures, from yeti-like golden snub-nosed monkeys in the mountain forests of China to a bizarre predator in the baking deserts of Iran

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Derek Mathewson has sold everything from trucks to bicycles - but he can't remember the last time he auctioned off a two-seater plane

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Tucked away in the showroom is a WW2-era Willys Jeep. Its original condition means there might be a bunfight for a military vehicle that's ripe for restoration

programme.desc: The Mathewsons take in a 1982 Audi Quattro and a Sunbeam Alpine. Plus, Derek takes delivery of a beautiful green Renault van, previously used as a bread van in Paris

programme.desc: Derek Mathewson has sold everything from trucks to bicycles - but he can't remember the last time he auctioned off a two-seater plane

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: 100 motorbikes come in for auction from every era of the 20th century, and the team assess a 1965 Vanden Plas Princess with a four litre Rolls Royce engine that's seen better days

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: How, in 1940, the French Army suffered a heavy defeat after underestimating the Nazis' strength while relying on First World War strategies and equipment to defend its frontiers. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: Secret Nazi Bases
programme.desc: A secret weapons program in the heart of Austria that could have ended badly for the Allies - and may have indicated nuclear plans being concocted by Hitler

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The team heads to Somerleyton Hall, near Lowestoft, where the objects up for valuation include a bracelet given to Queen Victoria and a picture painted by a suffragette artist. The experts also investigate the origins of an intriguing box of paints that is said to have belonged to John Constable

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming encounters a leaky lock pound on the Perry Barr Flight and gets marooned in the mud before travelling under Spaghetti Junction in Birmingham

programme.title: Train Truckers
programme.desc: The A4 LNER Pacific Locomotive, Sir Nigel Gresley, must be hauled 46 miles at 30mph from Crewe to Bridgnorth

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough turns his attention to life in Asia, the planet's most varied and extreme continent, which stretches from the Arctic Circle to the equator. Walruses gather in huge numbers in the frozen north and brown bears roam around remote Russian volcanoes, while the deep jungle provides sanctuary for the last few Sumatran rhino. There is also a glimpse of rarely seen creatures, from yeti-like golden snub-nosed monkeys in the mountain forests of China to a bizarre predator in the baking deserts of Iran

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Derek Mathewson has sold everything from trucks to bicycles - but he can't remember the last time he auctioned off a two-seater plane

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: 100 motorbikes come in for auction from every era of the 20th century, and the team assess a 1965 Vanden Plas Princess with a four litre Rolls Royce engine that's seen better days

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: There's a surprise for Derek in Hull - an MGSA sports car that once had a tree growing through it. Plus, in North Wales, Alan Holmes is selling his son's Ford Escort RS 1600i

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien visit Essex, where Henry restores a car trailer and a 1970s racing bike, and Simon turns his attention to lovingly restoring a vintage model aircraft

programme.title: Speed with Guy Martin
programme.desc: The motorbike racer takes part in four more speed-based challenges and gets his hands dirty in unique engineering projects to discover what makes things go fast. In the first programme, he finds out how far it's possible to travel on a bicycle during 24 hours of non-stop pedalling. With the help of bike experts Miles Kingsbury and Mike Burrows he builds a tandem, and pairs up with his friend, endurance expert Jason Miles, to see how far they can push their bodies. In the build-up to the final record attempt at the historic Goodwood Motor Circuit, Guy also learns about the science of cooking for athletes with nutritionist Nigel Mitchell, who instructs him to lose five kilos and recommends a new diet regimen

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Alex Polizzi heads to Crawley in West Sussex to help out a family-run concern just five miles away from Gatwick. Despite its proximity to the busy airport, the Waterhall Country House Hotel is struggling and owners Anne and Andy Marsh desperately need a change in fortunes if it is to become profitable. Can Alex set them on the way to success?

programme.title: Good Witch
programme.desc: Cassie displays Nathaniel Merriwick's paintings for Grey House's 225th birthday. Fantasy, starring Catherine Bell

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Early morning chat show presented by Lorraine Kelly, with celebrity interviews, entertainment news, cooking tips, fashion features and musical guests.

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: Celebrity interviews and studio discussion from a female perspective

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: Three singletons go up against each other in Manchester, where they try to win the affections of fashion stylist Courtney by each buying him an outfit

programme.title: In for a Penny
programme.desc: In this edition, Stephen Mulhern hosts the game show from York, setting members of the public unusual challenges for a chance to win anything from the price of their petrol to £1,000 in the big finale game

programme.title: In for a Penny
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts the game show from Bognor Regis, setting members of the public unusual challenges for a chance to win anything from the price of their petrol to £1,000 in the big finale game

programme.title: What Would Your Kid Do?
programme.desc: Children are filmed in a school specially rigged with hidden cameras. In the studio, host Jason Manford challenges parents to predict what their child will do in each situation, providing an insight into the developing minds of five to seven year-olds. The family with the most correct predictions goes through to the final. And the kids choose the prizes

programme.title: Yes Chef
programme.desc: Atul Kochhar puts three cooks through a series of culinary challenges, including how to make the perfect onion bhaji, choosing one of the amateurs to join him in the final

programme.desc: Chat show featuring celebrity interviews, food from leading chefs and guidance on a wide range of topics, from consumer affairs to the latest trends, with viewers invited to contribute with pictures, comments, stories and gossip

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, John, Kelly, Aimee and Ricky vie to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: The Instant Gardener
programme.desc: Helen Skelton and Danny Clarke head to Croydon, south London, to create exotic, Jamaican-style garden for owner Carmen, who needs a tranquil space to remember her lost daughter

programme.title: Peter Andre's 60 Minute Makeover
programme.desc: Peter and the team hit Hertfordshire, where single dad Rob Kircher gets the surprise of a lifetime as Linda Barker transforms his lounge and dining room with an American ranch theme, and turns his patio into an outdoor kitchen

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of the airport, finding out how its infrastructure helps staff process thousands of passengers arriving from 84 countries. This time, Security Officer Estefania investigates an oddly shaped object, and Passenger Experience Manager Harry deals with a medical emergency. Elsewhere, Border Force Bob helps a circus performer with visa troubles

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of the airport. This time, staff at the airport deal with the aftermath of a polar vortex. Meanwhile, officers Dave and Terry are on the hunt for a wanted man who has gone missing on a plane, and Ian and Steve battle in a snow blizzard as they attempt to keep the runways open

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: Examining a fleet of cruise ships that are the largest passenger vessels in the world, and revealing how they have built on 19th-century experiments in hydrodynamics

programme.title: Hotel Benidorm: Fun-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: New receptionist Ruben is about to experience his first unhappy customer as he works his first solo shift, while groom-to-be Dwain is whisked away from his stag do to get wedding-ready with fitness instructor Sarah. Also checking in today are two friends, with Michael treating his old pal JJ to a weekend away at their favourite holiday hotspot, and the pair's tickets aren't the only thing he booked

Alex Polizzi heads to north London to attempt to reverse Mel's fortunes

programme.title: Code of Silence
programme.desc: Alison's feelings for Liam threaten to undermine the police's investigation, but after learning he has been using her to mislead the cops about the heist by hacking her hearing aids, she races to the gang's true target of the Hawthorne Estate Hotel. programme.title: Ireland's Deadly Roads
programme.desc: Documentary examining the sharp rise in fatalities on the nation's roads, hearing from families of some of those who have died, who speak candidly about the lives lost, the ongoing grief, and their hopes that meaningful change can come from their experiences. The film investigates growing criticism of Ireland's road safety system, exploring concerns around lengthy delays in driving tests, the high number of learner drivers on the road, and whether current Essential Driver Training (EDT) requirements adequately reflect increasingly complex road conditions

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Early morning chat show presented by Lorraine Kelly, with celebrity interviews, entertainment news, cooking tips, fashion features and musical guests.

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: Another helping of topical studio discussion from a female perspective, featuring celebrity interviews

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: Real-life small-claims cases on issues affecting family life, presided over by outspoken former New York judge Judy Sheindlin

programme.title: Judge Judy

programme.title: News at 5.30
programme.desc: The latest national and international news stories from the Virgin Media News team

programme.title: The Six O'Clock Show
programme.desc: Chat show featuring celebrity interviews, food from leading chefs and guidance on a wide range of topics

programme.title: News at 7
programme.desc: The latest national and international news stories from the Virgin Media News team

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Rachel starts taking regular cigarette breaks to ingratiate herself with new boss Kim and colleague Nancy, even though she does not smoke, while Joey competes with Ross's son for a role in a TV commercial. Comedy, guest starring Joanna Gleason (Boogie Nights)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Laurel is feeling mortified, while Chas struggles to hold her tongue and Archie is left glowering

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Maria grows uneasy about Gary, while Kit keeps a close eye on him, Sam's remark puzzles Roy, Lily's family mark her sporting achievement, and Megan lures Will back into her orbit

programme.title: Ireland's Deadly Roads
programme.desc: Documentary examining the sharp rise in fatalities on the nation's roads, hearing from families of some of those who have died, who speak candidly about the lives lost, the ongoing grief, and their hopes that meaningful change can come from their experiences. The film investigates growing criticism of Ireland's road safety system, exploring concerns around lengthy delays in driving tests, the high number of learner drivers on the road, and whether current Essential Driver Training (EDT) requirements adequately reflect increasingly complex road conditions

programme.title: The Tonight Show
programme.desc: Comment and analysis on the big stories of the day in Ireland and abroad with a panel of guests covering the key issues

programme.title: The Game
programme.desc: Huw's promise to Alice to forget about Patrick and the Ripton Stalker comes under strain as his obsession grows. While pouring over the case, he finds a connection linking Ruth to Patrick in the form of a ballerina statue. Huw is surprised to see his daughter Margot back, and Alice kicks Huw out of the house when she discovers that he has lied to her

programme.title: McDonald & Dodds
programme.desc: The duo are sucked into the fast and furious world of Formula 1 after Bath's famous motorsport dynasty, the Addingtons, report that their up-and-coming driver has died during a sub-3.5-second pit stop. As an F1 fan herself, McDonald won't let patriarch and Team Coach Archie Addington run rings around her, but when a significant someone from her past gets dragged into the investigation, she's thrown off course. But it's one of Dodds' specialisms that leads them to uncover the darker side of success and finally unravel the truth

programme.title: Vigil
programme.desc: Kirsten uncovers evidence suggesting the murders were part of a coordinated attack. Her investigation takes her into the heart of Scottish politics, and the Naval cover up of a near catastrophe in the US. Amy starts to question who she can trust on Vigil, when a message from Kirsten makes Amy realise she is being deliberately undermined

programme.title: The Tonight Show
programme.desc: Comment and analysis on the big stories of the day in Ireland and abroad with a panel of guests covering the key issues

programme.title: Showtrial
programme.desc: Time is running out for Cleo and Talitha as the trial reaches its explosive conclusion. After the prosecution rests, Dhillon and Talitha each have the chance to give their account of what happened between them and Hannah. Meanwhile, Cleo and James both face hard decisions as they attempt to ensure a fair trial. Professional relationships are pushed to the limit, as James wrestles with his conscience, and Cleo chooses to defy Damian

programme.title: The Virtues
programme.desc: In the final episode, Joseph and Dinah finally get the answers they have been searching for, and they each have to decide whether to seek their own justice, or to forgive. Powerful four-part drama tackling themes of repressed memory, revenge and the hope of redemption, directed by Shane Meadows and co-written by Jack Thorne. Starring Stephen Graham and Niamh Algar

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The arrival of a traumatised and wounded stranger in Aidensfield coincides with the discovery of a woman's body on the moors, and sets off a treacherous chain of events that leaves one long-time resident's life hanging in the balance. Nicholas Farrell and Clare Holman guest star in the last-ever episode of the long-running police drama

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.sub-title: The Jewel Robbery at the Grand Metropolitan
programme.desc: On doctor's orders, the dapper Belgian sleuth visits Brighton for what he hopes will be a relaxing holiday, but it leads to one of the most absorbing cases he has ever faced - the disappearance of a cache of jewels. Mystery, starring David Suchet, with Hermione Norris, Philip Jackson and Sorcha Cusack

programme.title: Paddington Station 24/7
programme.desc: A summer heatwave threatens to cause major train delays across the network, and the British Transport Police request assistance to locate trespassers seen wandering close to the tracks

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: Lisa Faulkner makes a healthy and nourishing green goddess soup with garlic bread croutons and a one-tray roast chicken salad, which makes a nutritious lunch or dinner. Last up is John Torode's tangy and tempting roast pineapple with lime posset and crumble

programme.title: Peter Andre's 60 Minute Makeover
programme.desc: Peter Andre's 60 Minute Makeover is back and bigger than ever on Quest Red. Peter and his army of helpers will be surprising deserving people across the country with a stunning home mkeover - performed in a day and brought to you in 60 minutes. In each episode one deserving person will get the surprise home makeover of a lifetime. Nominated by their friends and family they are everyday heroes with moving stories that tug at the heart.

programme.title: Alex Polizzi: My Hotel Nightmare
programme.desc: There has been plenty of progress around the hotel, including the bedrooms in the sixties block and the new outside courtyard. Interior designer Olga can start to bring her design vision together, and even afford a little time away to shop for antiques. This leaves Alex to oversee the parts of the hotel that are of more concern

programme.title: Don't Look Back in Anger
programme.desc: Archive news documentary chronicling the highs and lows of 2022 and all the chaos in between

programme.title: Don't Look Back in Anger
programme.desc: Archive news documentary chronicling the highs and lows of 2023 and all the chaos in-between

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: The history of pioneering modern-day mega structures, revealing that behind every seemingly impossible marvel of modern engineering is a cast of historic trailblazers who designed new building techniques, took risks on untested materials and revolutionised their field

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Ruth Watson meets actor-hotelier Robert Gray before assessing his three-bedroom B&B in Greenwich, south-east London - a property whose lack of signage and overgrown garden make the front door hard to find. Established to maintain cash-flow between acting jobs, the former antiques dealer's property has been filled with unusual art and objects, and is in desperate need of attention

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Lydia's worst fears appear to be confirmed and she makes an accusation, while Kim spots that there is something between Ross and Laurel

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Long-running drama with the residents of England's most famous cobbled street

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: A wedding reception held on Elsinby Pier ends in tragedy when a fishing trawler sails badly off course. Meanwhile, Adam faces up to a tough decision and the staff at the hospital prepare for a new arrival as Jill goes into labour. Robert Cavanah and Amy Robbins star

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: Shaun Evans directs this episode, set against the backdrop of the first moon landings in 1969. The detective investigates the deaths of an astrophysicist and his girlfriend in a tragic car accident as the rest of the world eagerly awaits news from Apollo 11. When he begins to suspect foul play, Endeavour enlists Thursday's help in getting to the truth, visiting the observatory where the dead man worked and discovering the possibility that he was not the intended victim. The case is complicated further when the dead scientist is revealed to have been part of a mysterious cult-like group

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A flamboyant photographer and his models descend on the village, while Nick is called on to sort out a dispute at a hotel _ and finds himself looking down the barrel of a gun. Drama, starring Nick Berry and Derek Fowlds

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: Long-suffering DCI Vera Stanhope must unravel the mystery when well-respected local builder and family man Jim Tullman is found beaten to death on the steps of the Collingwood Monument. His demise happened just before he was due to testify in court as the key witness in a violent assault case. Crime drama, starring Brenda Blethyn, Kenny Doughty and Jon Morrison

programme.title: Only Fans: Ireland's X-Rated
programme.desc: Some Irish users revealed they earn over 50,000 euros a month on the subscriber platform, OnlyFans. This documentary follows the experience of some of the company's content creators

programme.title: Malpractice
programme.desc: The inquest into Edith Owusu's death begins, and Lucinda and her colleagues are under pressure from the hospital's management to protect their reputation. A pathologist reveals shocking evidence, and a surprise appearance from a familiar face leaves Lucinda reeling. Meanwhile, George and Norma investigate suspicious discoveries about Lucinda's previous patients - and uncover incriminating new evidence

programme.title: Don't Look Back in Anger
programme.desc: Archive news stories, looking back at the headlines from 1998

programme.title: Don't Look Back in Anger
programme.desc: Archive news stories from 2001, the year when two planes hit the Twin Towers in New York. It also saw the foot-and-mouth outbreak and Eamon Dunphy turn his hand to TV presenting

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A London policeman and his doctor wife encounter hostility and suspicion when they move to a close-knit Yorkshire community, but feuding mods and rockers, a mysterious prowler and a budgie-killer mean the couple soon find themselves in the thick of village life. First-ever episode of the popular Sixties-set drama, starring Nick Berry, Niamh Cusack, Derek Fowlds and Bill Maynard

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: The naked bodies of three men are found placed in the shape of a triangle, leading Jack to suspect the work of a satanic cult. Meanwhile, press baron James Callum's plans to make his mark on Denton are hindered by an investigation into his lover's death in a houseboat fire - and the suspicious demise of his former girlfriend may be the key to unmasking the killer. Starring David Jason, Bruce Alexander, Adrian Lukis, Sarah Matravers and Paul Shane

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner make dishes perfect for picnics, including simple jam and treacle mini tarts, salmon and cucumber pinwheels, and sausage rolls. Finally, they bring together beautiful spices with a take on simple Bombay potatoes, a chickpea dahl and oven-baked samosas

In each episode one deserving person will get the surprise home makeover of a lifetime. Nominated by their friends and family they are everyday heroes with moving stories that tug at the heart.

programme.title: Paddington Station 24/7
programme.desc: A fire alarm causes Paddington Station to be evacuated in rush hour, and soaring temperatures sees disruption to the network across the busy Bank Holiday weekend

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A flamboyant photographer and his models descend on the village, while Nick is called on to sort out a dispute at a hotel _ and finds himself looking down the barrel of a gun. Drama, starring Nick Berry and Derek Fowlds

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: Experts use cutting-edge tech and bold engineering to build a record-breaking bridge that spans a deadly chasm in a remote section of the Himalayas. When completed, it will be the world's tallest railway bridge, towering almost 1,200 feet high

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Ruth Watson meets novice hoteliers Elly Koopman and Rob Farrow and assesses their nine-bedroom Haven Hotel in Great Yarmouth, Norfolk. Bought only four months prior to Ruth's arrival, the establishment has left the pair out of their depth and desperately in need of a crash course in running a hotel

programme.title: The Instant Gardener
programme.desc: Presenter Helen Skelton and expert garden designer Danny Clarke bring unloved gardens back to life in a single day using clever design, imaginative ideas and recycling or re-use of existing plants and materials. They begin by travelling to Blackpool to transform Paul and Sarah's boggy, waterlogged plot

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: The train on which Marian is travelling crashes, trapping her in the wreckage. Although she cannot escape, she's free to move around the carriage to help those injured, assisted by fellow passenger Dr Nick Burnett - who turns out to be the Royal's new locum. Back at the hospital, a shortage of staff puts the casualty department under pressure when the crash victims begin to arrive. Damian O'Hare joins the cast, and Gerard Horan guest stars

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: The detective is assigned to investigate when a chocolate factory owner is murdered during a fox hunt. The case leads him to the sleepy Oxfordshire village of Chigton Green, and is soon complicated by the fatal shooting of a local couple with an apparent connection to the first victim. Endeavour uncovers a vicious campaign of gossip and rumour, and meets a local woman who inspires him to consider his future

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: An old pal of Greengrass's turns up with a money-making scheme, but the pair are soon out of their depth and Nick is forced to go undercover to sort it out. Meanwhile, Ventress tries to organise a marquee for his daughter's wedding - with disappointing results. Police drama, starring Nick Berry

programme.title: Live International Football
programme.desc: England v Costa Rica (Kick-off 9.00pm). All the action from the friendly at Inter&Co Stadium in Orlando, Florida. This is England's final warm-up game before they get their World Cup campaign under way against Croatia in Dallas on 17th June. They will be expecting a morale-boosting victory here against a team that have failed to qualify for the finals for the first time since 2010, and they prevailed 2-0 when the sides last met at Elland Road in 2018 thanks to goals by Marcus Rashford and Danny Welbeck

programme.title: Seconds to Spare (2002)
programme.desc: A former government agent is shocked to discover his arch-enemy has infiltrated a gang of extreme environmentalists - and is bent on contaminating the world with a deadly nerve gas unless he's paid a ransom. Thriller, starring Jerome Ehlers, Antonio Sabato Jnr, Kimberly Davies and Kate Beahan

programme.title: The Real Housewives of New Jersey
programme.desc: US reality series. Day one at the Jersey Shore begins with a luau that takes a turn for the worse, and Rachel drops a load gossip that angers Marge.

programme.title: After the Party
programme.desc: Penny tries to apologise to Grace and Phil, but learns that they're moving to Scotland. When Ollie goes missing, she asks Tom to look for him but doesn't realise what this really means for him. Penny makes a new discovery and finally sees what was under her nose the whole time. At Grace's farewell party, Penny's relationship with her daughter finally begins to heal, but only because Penny is suppressing what she now knows is the truth. She tries to get to Phil before Tom in a bid to prevent the consequences of actions from the past. New Zealand-set thriller, starring Robyn Malcolm and Peter Mullan

programme.title: Winning Combination
programme.desc: Quiz, hosted by Omid Djalili, in which contestants Rory, Donna, Julie, Sharron, David, Steve, Jenny, Gill and Stu are assigned a number between one and nine. They are then whittled down to a final four, and the combination of numbers they are assigned represents the size of the jackpot they play to win in a final round

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: All that stands between Mark from Liverpool, Rachael from Lytham St Annes, Issmyle from Nottingham and Kate from Cambridge and thousands of pounds is the not-so-small matter of one of the country's finest quiz brains. Bradley Walsh asks the questions, but do they have the answers?

Duncan and Glen, Sophie and Molly and Kay and Hashim take part in this edition

programme.desc: Ross joins Rachel on her first trip to a launderette, where a machine shortage leads to a tense stand-off. Phoebe and Chandler make a pact to break up with their partners, and Joey tricks a former girlfriend into believing Monica is the new woman in his life

programme.desc: Louie Spence plays mystery host as he throws a dinner party for four strangers at his home. programme.title: Live Athletics: Diamond League
programme.desc: The Bislett Games. Coverage of the sixth meeting of the campaign, held at Bislett Stadium in Oslo, Norway.
